             streams application configuration to read the root state directory path\n   135→     * @param time                system timer used to execute periodic cleanup procedure\n   136→     * @param hasPersistentStores only when the application's topology does have stores persisted on local file\n   137→     *                            system, we would go ahead and auto-create the corresponding application / task / store\n   138→     *                            directories whenever necessary; otherwise no directories would be created.\n   139→     * @param hasNamedTopologies  whether this application is composed of independent named topologies\n   140→     *\n   141→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the base state directory or application state directory does not exist\n   142→     *                                 and could not be created when hasPersistentStores is enabled.\n   143→     */\n   144→    public StateDirectory(final StreamsConfig config, final Time time, final boolean hasPersistentStores, final boolean hasNamedTopologies) {\n   145→        this.time = time;\n   146→        this.hasPersistentStores = hasPersistentStores;\n   147→        this.hasNamedTopologies = hasNamedTopologies;\n   148→        this.appId = config.getString(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG);\n   149→        this.config = config;\n   150→        final String stateDirName = config.getString(StreamsConfig.STATE_DIR_CONFIG);\n   151→        final File baseDir = new File(stateDirName);\n   152→        stateDir = new File(baseDir, appId);\n   153→\n   154→        if (this.hasPersistentStores) {\n   155→            if (!baseDir.exists() && !baseDir.mkdirs()) {\n   156→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   157→                    String.format(\"base state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDirName));\n   158→            }\n   159→            if (!stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.mkdir()) {\n   160→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   161→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   162→            } else if (stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.isDirectory()) {\n   163→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   164→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   165→            }\n   166→\n   167→            if (stateDirName.startsWith(System.getProperty(\"java.io.tmpdir\"))) {\n   168→                log.warn(\"Using an OS temp directory in the state.dir property can cause failures with writing\" +\n   169→                    \" the checkpoint file due to the fact that this directory can be cleared by the OS.\" +\n   170→                    \" Resolved state.dir: [\" + stateDirName + \"]\");\n   171→\n   172→            }\n   173→            // change the dir permission to \"rwxr-x---\" to avoid world readable\n   174→            configurePermissions(baseDir);\n   175→            configurePermissions(stateDir);\n   176→        }\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    private void configurePermissions(final File file) {\n   180→        final Path path = file.toPath();\n   181→        if (path.getFileSystem().supportedFileAttributeViews().contains(\"posix\")) {\n   182→            final Set<PosixFilePermission> perms = EnumSet.of(\n   183→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,\n   184→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,\n   185→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE,\n   186→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_READ,\n   187→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_EXECUTE\n   188→            );\n   189→            if (config.getBoolean(StreamsConfig.ALLOW_OS_GROUP_WRITE_ACCESS_CONFIG)) {\n   190→                perms.add(PosixFilePermission.GROUP_WRITE);\n   191→            }\n   192→            try {\n   193→                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(path, perms);\n   194→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   195→                log.error(\"Error changing permissions for the directory {} \", path, e);\n   196→            }\n   197→        } else {\n   198→            boolean set = file.setReadable(true, true);\n   199→            set &= file.setWritable(true, true);\n   200→            set &= file.setExecutable(true, true);\n   201→            if (!set) {\n   202→                log.error(\"Failed to change permissions for the directory {}\", file);\n   203→            }\n   204→        }\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    /**\n   208→     * @return true if the state directory was successfully locked\n   209→     */\n   210→    private boolean lockStateDirectory() {\n   211→        final File lockFile = new File(stateDir, LOCK_FILE_NAME);\n   212→        try {\n   213→            stateDirLockChannel = FileChannel.open(lockFile.toPath(), StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.WRITE);\n   214→            stateDirLock = tryLock(stateDirLockChannel);\n   215→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   216→            log.error(\"Unable to lock the state directory due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   217→            throw new ProcessorStateException(String.format(\"Failed to lock the state directory [%s] during startup\",\n   218→                stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   219→        }\n   220→        return stateDirLock != null;\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * @throws 227→    public void initializeStartupStores(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n   228→                                        final LogContext logContext,\n   229→                                        final StreamsMetricsImpl metricsImpl) {\n   230→        final List<TaskDirectory> nonEmptyTaskDirectories = listNonEmptyTaskDirectories();\n   231→        if (hasPersistentStores && !nonEmptyTaskDirectories.isEmpty()) {\n   232→            final boolean eosEnabled = StreamsConfigUtils.eosEnabled(config);\n   233→\n   234→            // Initialize thread-specific resources needed to open stores in the state directory\n   235→            final String threadLogPrefix = String.format(\"[%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   236→            final ThreadCache dummyCache = new ThreadCache(new LogContext(threadLogPrefix), 0L, metricsImpl);\n   237→\n   238→            // discover all non-empty task directories in StateDirectory\n   239→            for (final TaskDirectory taskDirectory : nonEmptyTaskDirectories) {\n   240→                final String dirName = taskDirectory.file().getName();\n   241→                final TaskId task = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDirectory.namedTopology());\n   242→                final ProcessorTopology subTopology = topologyMetadata.buildSubtopology(task);\n   243→\n   244→                // we still check if the task's sub-topology is stateful, even though we know its directory contains state,\n   245→                // because it's possible that the topology has changed since that data was written, and is now stateless\n   246→                // this therefore prevents us from creating unnecessary stores just because of some left-over state\n   247→                if (subTopology.hasStateWithChangelogs()) {\n   248→                    final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions = topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task).values().stream()\n   249→                            .flatMap(Collection::stream)\n   250→                            .map(t -> new TopicPartition(t, task.partition()))\n   251→                            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   252→                    // Open a temporary state manager that will open the stores inside the subtopology\n   253→                    final ProcessorStateManager temporaryStateManager = ProcessorStateManager.createStartupTaskStateManager(\n   254→                        task,\n   255→                        eosEnabled,\n   256→                        logContext,\n   257→                        this,\n   258→                        subTopology.storeToChangelogTopic(),\n   259→                        inputPartitions\n   260→                    );\n   261→\n   262→                    final StartupContext initContext = new StartupContext(task, config, temporaryStateManager, metricsImpl, dummyCache);\n   263→                    try {\n   264→                        // We only handle TaskCorruptedException at this point. Any other exception is considered fatal.\n   265→                        StateManagerUtil.registerStateStores(log, threadLogPrefix, subTopology, temporaryStateManager, this, initContext);\n   266→                        temporaryStateManager.checkpoint();\n   267→                    } catch (final TaskCorruptedException tce) {\n   268→                        // At this point, we only log a warning and continue with the startup store initialization.\n   269→                        // The task-corrupted exception will be handled in the first Task assignment phase.\n   270→                        log.warn(\"Failed to register startup state stores for task {}: {}\", task, tce.getMessage());\n   271→                    } finally {\n   272→                        // Make sure the state manager writes the local checkpoint file before closing the stores\n   273→                        // This will be replaced in the future when removing the checkpoint file dependency.\n   274→                        temporaryStateManager.close();\n   275→                    }\n   276→                    tasksInLocalState.add(task);\n   277→                }\n   278→            }\n   279→        }\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    public boolean hasStartupTasks() {\n   283→        return !tasksInLocalState.isEmpty();\n   284→    }\n   285→\n   286→    public synchronized boolean removeStartupState(final TaskId taskId) {\n   287→        final boolean removed = tasksInLocalState.remove(taskId);\n   288→        if (removed) {\n   289→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   290→        }\n   291→        return removed;\n   292→    }\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→    private void unlockStartupStores() {\n   296→        for (final TaskId task : tasksInLocalState) {\n   297→            unlock(task);\n   298→        }\n   299→    }\n   300→\n   301→    public Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums(final Set<TaskId> tasks) {\n   302→        return taskOffsetSums.entrySet().stream()\n   303→                .filter(e -> tasks.contains(e.getKey()))\n   304→                .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   305→    }\n   306→\n   307→    public void updateTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   308→        if (!changelogOffsets.isEmpty()) {\n   309→            taskOffsetSums.put(taskId, sumOfChangelogOffsets(taskId, changelogOffsets));\n   310→        }\n   311→    }\n   312→\n   313→    public void removeTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId) {\n   314→        taskOffsetSums.remove(taskId);\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    private long sumOfChangelogOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   318→        long offsetSum = 0L;\n   319→        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> changelogEntry : changelogOffsets.entrySet()) {\n   320→            final long offset = changelogEntry.getValue();\n   321→\n   322→            if (offset != OffsetCheckpoint.OFFSET_UNKNOWN) {\n   323→                if (offset < 0) {\n   324→                    throw new StreamsException(\n   325→                            new IllegalStateException(\"Expected not to get a sentinel offset, but got: \" + changelogEntry),\n   326→                            taskId);\n   327→                }\n   328→                offsetSum += offset;\n   329→                if (offsetSum < 0) {\n   330→                    log.warn(\"Sum of changelog offsets for task {} overflowed, pinning to Long.MAX_VALUE\", taskId);\n   331→                    return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   332→                }\n   333→            }\n   334→        }\n   335→\n   336→        return offsetSum;\n   337→    }\n   338→\n   339→    public UUID initializeProcessId() {\n   340→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   341→            final UUID processId = UUID.randomUUID();\n   342→            log.info(\"Created new process id: {}\", processId);\n   343→            return processId;\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        if (!lockStateDirectory()) {\n   347→            log.error(\"Unable to obtain lock as state directory is already locked by another process\");\n   348→            throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Unable to initialize state, this can happen if multiple instances of \" +\n   349→                                           \"Kafka Streams are running in the same state directory \" +\n   350→                                           \"(current state directory is [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()));\n   351→        }\n   352→\n   353→        final File processFile = new File(stateDir, PROCESS_FILE_NAME);\n   354→        final ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();\n   355→\n   356→        try {\n   357→            if (processFile.exists()) {\n   358→                try {\n   359→                    final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = mapper.readValue(processFile, StateDirectoryProcessFile.class);\n   360→                    log.info(\"Reading UUID from process file: {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   361→                    if (processFileData.processId != null) {\n   362→                        return processFileData.processId;\n   363→                    }\n   364→                } catch (final Exception e) {\n   365→                    log.warn(\"Failed to read json process file\", e);\n   366→                }\n   367→            }\n   368→\n   369→            final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = new StateDirectoryProcessFile(UUID.randomUUID());\n   370→            log.info(\"No process id found on disk, got fresh process id {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   371→\n   372→            mapper.writeValue(processFile, processFileData);\n   373→            return processFileData.processId;\n   374→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   375→            log.error(\"Unable to read/write process file due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   376→            throw new ProcessorStateException(e);\n   377→        }\n   378→    }\n   379→\n   380→    /**\n   381→     * Get or create the directory for the provided {@link TaskId}.\n   382→     * @return directory for the {@link TaskId}\n   383→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   384→     */\n   385→    public File getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(final TaskId taskId) {\n   386→        final File taskParentDir = getTaskDirectoryParentName(taskId);\n   387→        final File taskDir = new File(taskParentDir, StateManagerUtil.toTaskDirString(taskId));\n   388→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   389→            if (!taskDir.exists()) {\n   390→                synchronized (taskDirCreationLock) {\n   391→                    // to avoid a race condition, we need to check again if the directory does not exist:\n   392→                    // otherwise, two threads might pass the outer `if` (and enter the `then` block),\n   393→                    // one blocks on `synchronized` while the other creates the directory,\n   394→                    // and the blocking one fails when trying to create it after it's unblocked\n   395→                    if (!taskParentDir.exists() && !taskParentDir.mkdir()) {\n   396→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   397→                            String.format(\"Parent [%s] of task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\",\n   398→                                taskParentDir.getPath(), taskDir.getPath()));\n   399→                    }\n   400→                    if (!taskDir.exists() && !taskDir.mkdir()) {\n   401→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   402→                            String.format(\"task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   403→                    }\n   404→                }\n   405→            } else if (!taskDir.isDirectory()) {\n   406→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   407→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   408→            }\n   409→        }\n   410→        return taskDir;\n   411→    }\n   412→\n   413→    private File getTaskDirectoryParentName(final TaskId taskId) {\n   414→        final String namedTopology = taskId.topologyName();\n   415→        if (namedTopology != null) {\n   416→            if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   417→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Tried to lookup taskId with named topology, but StateDirectory thinks hasNamedTopologies = false\");\n   418→            }\n   419→            return new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(namedTopology));\n   420→        } else {\n   421→            return stateDir;\n   422→        }\n   423→    }\n   424→\n   425→    private String getNamedTopologyDirName(final String topologyName) {\n   426→        return \"__\" + topologyName + \"__\";\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    /**\n   430→     * @return The File handle for the checkpoint in the given task's directory\n   431→     */\n   432→    File checkpointFileFor(final TaskId taskId) {\n   433→        return new File(getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId), StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME);\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /**\n   437→     * Decide if the directory of the task is empty or not\n   438→     */\n   439→    boolean directoryForTaskIsEmpty(final TaskId taskId) {\n   440→        final File taskDir = getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId);\n   441→\n   442→        return taskDirIsEmpty(taskDir);\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    private boolean taskDirIsEmpty(final File taskDir) {\n   446→        final File[] storeDirs = taskDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   447→                !pathname.getName().equals(CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME));\n   448→\n   449→        boolean taskDirEmpty = true;\n   450→\n   451→        // if the task is stateless, storeDirs would be null\n   452→        if (storeDirs != null && storeDirs.length > 0) {\n   453→            for (final File file : storeDirs) {\n   454→                // We removed the task directory locking but some upgrading applications may still have old lock files on disk,\n   455→                // we just lazily delete those in this method since it's the only thing that would be affected by these\n   456→                if (file.getName().endsWith(LOCK_FILE_NAME)) {\n   457→                    if (!file.delete()) {\n   458→                        // If we hit an error deleting this just ignore it and move on, we'll retry again at some point\n   459→                        log.warn(\"Error encountered deleting lock file in {}\", taskDir);\n   460→                    }\n   461→                } else {\n   462→                    // If it's not a lock file then the directory is not empty,\n   463→                    // but finish up the loop in case there's a lock file left to delete\n   464→                    log.trace(\"TaskDir {} was not empty, found {}\", taskDir, file);\n   465→                    taskDirEmpty = false;\n   466→                }\n   467→            }\n   468→        }\n   469→        return taskDirEmpty;\n   470→    }\n   471→\n   472→    /**\n   473→     * Get or create the directory for the global stores.\n   474→     * @return directory for the global stores\n   475→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the global store directory does not exists and could not be created\n   476→     */\n   477→    File globalStateDir() {\n   478→        final File dir = new File(stateDir, \"global\");\n   479→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   480→            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdir()) {\n   481→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   482→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", dir.getPath()));\n   483→            } else if (dir.exists() && !dir.isDirectory()) {\n   484→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   485→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", dir.getPath()));\n   486→            }\n   487→        }\n   488→        return dir;\n   489→    }\n   490→\n   491→    private String logPrefix() {\n   492→        return String.format(\"stream-thread [%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   493→    }\n   494→\n   495→    /**\n   496→     * Get the lock for the {@link TaskId}s directory if it is available\n   497→     * @param taskId task id\n   498→     * @return true if successful\n   499→     */\n   500→    synchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   501→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   502→            return true;\n   503→        }\n   504→\n   505→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   506→        if (lockOwner != null) {\n   507→            if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   508→                log.trace(\"{} Found cached state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   509→                // we already own the lock\n   510→                return true;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                // another thread owns the lock\n   513→                return false;\n   514→            }\n   515→        } else if (!stateDir.exists()) {\n   516→            log.error(\"Tried to lock task directory for {} but the state directory does not exist\", taskId);\n   517→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"The state directory has been deleted\");\n   518→        } else {\n   519→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   520→            return true;\n   521→        }\n   522→    }\n   523→\n   524→    /**\n   525→     * Unlock the state directory for the given {@link TaskId}.\n   526→     */\n   527→    synchronized void unlock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   528→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   529→        if (lockOwner != null && lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   530→            lockedTasksToOwner.remove(taskId);\n   531→            log.debug(\"{} Released state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   532→        }\n   533→    }\n   534→\n   535→    /**\n   536→     * Expose for tests.\n   537→     */\n   538→    Thread lockOwner(final TaskId taskId) {\n   539→        return lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   540→    }\n   541→\n   542→    @Override\n   543→    public void close() {\n   544→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   545→            unlockStartupStores();\n   546→            taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   547→            try {\n   548→                stateDirLock.release();\n   549→                stateDirLockChannel.close();\n   550→\n   551→                stateDirLock = null;\n   552→                stateDirLockChannel = null;\n   553→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   554→                log.error(\"Unexpected exception while unlocking the state dir\", e);\n   555→                throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Failed to release the lock on the state directory [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   556→            }\n   557→\n   558→            // all threads should be stopped and cleaned up by now, so none should remain holding a lock\n   559→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   560→                log.error(\"Some task directories still locked while closing state, this indicates unclean shutdown: {}\", lockedTasksToOwner);\n   561→            }\n   562→        }\n   563→    }\n   564→\n   565→    public synchronized void clean() {\n   566→        try {\n   567→            cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser();\n   568→        } catch (final Exception e) {\n   569→            throw new StreamsException(e);\n   570→        }\n   571→\n   572→        try {\n   573→            if (stateDir.exists()) {\n   574→                Utils.delete(globalStateDir().getAbsoluteFile());\n   575→            }\n   576→        } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   577→            log.error(\n   578→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete global state directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   579→                    logPrefix(), appId),\n   580→                exception\n   581→            );\n   582→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   583→        }\n   584→\n   585→        try {\n   586→            if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.delete()) {\n   587→                log.warn(\n   588→                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s for it is not empty\",\n   589→                        logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath())\n   590→                );\n   591→            }\n   592→        } catch (final SecurityException exception) {\n   593→            log.error(\n   594→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   595→                    logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath()),\n   596→                exception\n   597→            );\n   598→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   599→        }\n   600→    }\n   601→\n   602→    /**\n   603→     * Remove the directories for any {@link TaskId}s that are no-longer\n   604→     * owned by this {@link StreamThread} and aren't locked by either\n   605→     * another process or another {@link StreamThread}\n   606→     * @param cleanupDelayMs only remove directories if they haven't been modified for at least\n   607→     *                       this amount of time (milliseconds)\n   608→     */\n   609→    public synchronized void cleanRemovedTasks(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   610→        try {\n   611→            cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(cleanupDelayMs);\n   612→        } catch (final Exception cannotHappen) {\n   613→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Should have swallowed exception.\", cannotHappen);\n   614→        }\n   615→    }\n   616→\n   617→    private void cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   618→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   619→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   620→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n   621→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n   622→                try {\n   623→                    if (lock(id)) {\n   624→                        final long now = time.milliseconds();\n   625→                        final long lastModifiedMs = taskDir.file().lastModified();\n   626→                        if (now - cleanupDelayMs > lastModifiedMs) {\n   627→                            removeTaskOffsets(id);\n   628→                            log.info(\"{} Deleting obsolete state directory {} for task {} as {}ms has elapsed (cleanup delay is {}ms).\",\n   629→                                logPrefix(), dirName, id, now - lastModifiedMs, cleanupDelayMs);\n   630→                            removeStartupState(id);\n   631→                            Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n   632→                        }\n   633→                    }\n   634→                } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   635→                    log.warn(\n   636→                        String.format(\"%s Swallowed the following exception during deletion of obsolete state directory %s for task %s:\",\n   637→                            logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n   638→                        exception\n   639→                    );\n   640→                } finally {\n   641→                    unlock(id);\n   642→                }\n   643→            }\n   644→        }\n   645→        // Ok to ignore returned exception as it should be swallowed\n   646→        maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(true);\n   647→    }\n   648→\n   649→    /**\n   650→     * Cleans up any leftover named topology directories that are empty, if any exist\n   651→     * @param logExceptionAsWarn if true, an exception will be logged as a warning\n   652→     *                       if false, an exception will be logged as error\n   653→     * @return the first IOException to be encountered\n   654→     */\n   655→    private IOException maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(final boolean logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   656→        if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   657→            return null;\n   658→        }\n   659→\n   660→        final AtomicReference<IOException> firstException = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n   661→        final File[] namedTopologyDirs = stateDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   662→                pathname.isDirectory() && NAMED_TOPOLOGY_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()\n   663→        );\n   664→        if (namedTopologyDirs != null) {\n   665→            for (final File namedTopologyDir : namedTopologyDirs) {\n   666→                final String topologyName = parseNamedTopologyFromDirectory(namedTopologyDir.getName());\n   667→                final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n   668→                taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n   669→                final File[] contents = namedTopologyDir.listFiles();\n   670→                if (contents != null && contents.length == 0) {\n   671→                    try {\n   672→                        Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n   673→                    } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   674→                        if (logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   675→                            log.warn(\n   676→                                String.format(\"%sSwallowed the following exception during deletion of named topology directory %s\",\n   677→                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n   678→                                exception\n   679→                            );\n   680→                        } else {\n   681→                            log.error(\n   682→                                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete named topology directory %s with exception:\",\n   683→                                    logPrefix(), namedTopologyDir.getName()),\n   684→                                exception\n   685→                            );\n   686→                        }\n   687→                        firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   688→                    }\n   689→                }\n   690→            }\n   691→        }\n   692→        return firstException.get();\n   693→    }\n   694→\n   695→    /**\n   696→     * Clears out any local state found for the given NamedTopology after it was removed\n   697→     *\n   698→     * @throws StreamsException if cleanup failed\n   699→     */\n   700→    public void clearLocalStateForNamedTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   701→        final File namedTopologyDir = new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(topologyName));\n   702→        if (!namedTopologyDir.exists() || !namedTopologyDir.isDirectory()) {\n   703→            log.debug(\"Tried to clear out the local state for NamedTopology {} but none was found\", topologyName);\n   704→        }\n   705→        try {\n   706→            final Set<TaskId> taskKeys = taskOffsetSums.keySet();\n   707→            taskKeys.removeIf(taskId -> taskId.topologyName().equals(topologyName));\n   708→            Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n   709→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   710→            log.error(\"Hit an unexpected error while clearing local state for topology \" + topologyName, e);\n   711→            throw new StreamsException(\"Unable to delete state for the named topology \" + topologyName,\n   712→                                       e, new TaskId(-1, -1, topologyName)); // use dummy taskid to report source topology for this error\n   713→        }\n   714→    }\n   715→\n   716→    private void cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser() throws Exception {\n   717→        if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   718→            log.warn(\"Found some still-locked task directories when user requested to cleaning up the state, \"\n   719→                + \"since Streams is not running any more these will be ignored to complete the cleanup\");\n   720→        }\n   721→        taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   722→        final AtomicReference<Exception> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n   723→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   724→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   725→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n   726→            try {\n   727→                log.info(\"{} Deleting task directory {} for {} as user calling cleanup.\",\n   728→                    logPrefix(), dirName, id);\n   729→\n   730→                if (lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n   731→                    log.warn(\"{} Task {} in state directory {} was still locked by {}\",\n   732→                        logPrefix(), dirName, id, lockedTasksToOwner.get(id));\n   733→                }\n   734→                Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n   735→            } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   736→                log.error(\n   737→                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete task directory %s for %s with exception:\",\n   738→                        logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n   739→                    exception\n   740→                );\n   741→                firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   742→            }\n   743→        }\n   744→\n   745→        firstException.compareAndSet(null, maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(false));\n   746→\n   747→        final Exception exception = firstException.get();\n   748→        if (exception != null) {\n   749→            throw exception;\n   750→        }\n   751→    }\n   752→\n   753→    /**\n   754→     * List all of the task directories that are non-empty\n   755→     * @return The list of all the non-empty local directories for stream tasks\n   756→     */\n   757→    List<TaskDirectory> listNonEmptyTaskDirectories() {\n   758→        return listTaskDirectories(pathname -> {\n   759→            if (!pathname.isDirectory() || !TASK_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()) {\n   760→                return false;\n   761→            } else {\n   762→                return !taskDirIsEmpty(pathname);\n   763→            }\n   764→        });\n   765→    }\n   766→\n   767→    /**\n   768→     * List all of the task directories along with their parent directory if they belong to a named topology\n   769→     * @return The list of all the existing local directories for stream tasks\n   770→     */\n   771→    List<TaskDirectory> listAllTaskDirectories() {\n   772→        return listTaskDirectories(pathname -> pathname.isDirectory() && TASK_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches());\n   773→    }\n   774→\n   775→    private List<TaskDirectory> listTaskDirectories(final FileFilter filter) {\n   776→        final List<TaskDirectory> taskDirectories = new ArrayList<>();\n   777→        if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists()) {\n   778→            if (hasNamedTopologies) {\n   779→                for (final File namedTopologyDir : listNamedTopologyDirs()) {\n   780→                    final String namedTopology = org.apache.kafka.streams.internals.ApiUtils.prepareMillisCheckFailMsgPrefix;\n   115→import static org.apache.kafka.streams.internals.ApiUtils.validateMillisecondDuration;\n   116→import static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TopologyMetadata.UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY;\n   119→\n   120→/**\n   121→ * A Kafka client that allows for performing continuous computation on input coming from one or more input topics and\n   122→ * sends output to zero, one, or more output topics.\n   123→ * <p>\n   124→ * The computational logic can be specified either by using the {@link Topology} to define a DAG topology of\n   125→ * {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.api.Processor}s or by using the {@link StreamsBuilder} which provides the high-level DSL to define\n   126→ * transformations.\n   127→ * <p>\n   128→ * One {@code KafkaStreams} instance can contain one or more threads specified in the configs for the processing work.\n   129→ * <p>\n   130→ * A {@code KafkaStreams} instance can co-ordinate with any other instances with the same\n   131→ * {@link StreamsConfig#APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG application ID} (whether in the same process, on other processes on this\n   132→ * machine, or on remote machines) as a single (possibly distributed) stream processing application.\n   133→ * These instances will divide up the work based on the assignment of the input topic partitions so that all partitions\n   134→ * are being consumed.\n   135→ * If instances are added or fail, all (remaining) instances will rebalance the partition assignment among themselves\n   136→ * to balance processing load and ensure that all input topic partitions are processed.\n   137→ * <p>\n   138→ * Internally a {@code KafkaStreams} instance contains a normal {@link KafkaProducer} and {@link KafkaConsumer} instance\n   139→ * that is used for reading input and writing output.\n   140→ * <p>\n   141→ * A simple example might look like this:\n   142→ * <pre>{@code\n   143→ * Properties props = new Properties();\n   144→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG, \"my-stream-processing-application\");\n   145→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n   146→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_KEY_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n   147→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_VALUE_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n   148→ *\n   149→ * StreamsBuilder builder = new StreamsBuilder();\n   150→ * builder.<String, String>stream(\"my-input-topic\").mapValues(value -> String.valueOf(value.length())).to(\"my-output-topic\");\n   151→ *\n   152→ * KafkaStreams streams = new KafkaStreams(builder.build(), props);\n   153→ * streams.start();\n   154→ * }</pre>\n   155→ *\n   156→ * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.StreamsBuilder\n   157→ * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.Topology\n   158→ */\n   159→public class KafkaStreams implements AutoCloseable {\n   160→\n   161→    private static final String JMX_PREFIX = \"kafka.streams\";\n   162→\n   163→    // processId is expected to be unique across JVMs and to be used\n   164→    // in userData of the subscription request to allow assignor be aware\n   165→    // of the co-location of stream thread's consumers. It is for internal\n   166→    // usage only and should not be exposed to users at all.\n   167→    private final Time time;\n   168→    private final Logger log;\n   169→    protected final String clientId;\n   170→    private final Metrics metrics;\n   171→    protected final StreamsConfig applicationConfigs;\n   172→    protected final List<StreamThread> threads;\n   173→    protected final StreamsMetadataState streamsMetadataState;\n   174→    private final ScheduledExecutorService stateDirCleaner;\n   175→    private final ScheduledExecutorService rocksDBMetricsRecordingService;\n   176→    protected final Admin adminClient;\n   177→    private final StreamsMetricsImpl streamsMetrics;\n   178→    private final long totalCacheSize;\n   179→    private final StreamStateListener streamStateListener;\n   180→    private final DelegatingStateRestoreListener delegatingStateRestoreListener;\n   181→    private final UUID processId;\n   182→    private final KafkaClientSupplier DelegatingStandbyUpdateListener delegatingStandbyUpdateListener;\n   186→    private final LogContext logContext;\n   187→\n   188→    GlobalStreamThread globalStreamThread;\n   189→    protected StateDirectory stateDirectory = null;\n   190→    private KafkaStreams.StateListener stateListener;\n   191→    private BiConsumer<Throwable, Boolean> streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler;\n   192→    private final Object changeThreadCount = new Object();\n   193→\n   194→    // container states\n   195→    /**\n   196→     * Kafka Streams states are the possible state that a Kafka Streams instance can be in.\n   197→     * An instance must only be in one state at a time.\n   198→     * The expected state transition with the following defined states is:\n   199→     *\n   200→     * <pre>\n   201→     *                 +--------------+\n   202→     *         +&lt;----- | Created (0)  |\n   203→     *         |       +-----+--------+\n   204→     *         |             |\n   205→     *         |             v\n   206→     *         |       +----+--+------+\n   207→     *         |       | Re-          |\n   208→     *         +&lt;----- | Balancing (1)| --------&gt;+\n   209→     *         |       +-----+-+------+          |\n   210→     *         |             | ^                 |\n   211→     *         |             v |                 |\n   212→     *         |       +--------------+          v\n   213→     *         |       | Running (2)  | --------&gt;+\n   214→     *         |       +------+-------+          |\n   215→     *         |              |                  |\n   216→     *         |              v                  |\n   217→     *         |       +------+-------+     +----+-------+\n   218→     *         +-----&gt; | Pending      |     | Pending    |\n   219→     *                 | Shutdown (3) |     | Error (5)  |\n   220→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+------+\n   221→     *                        |                   |\n   222→     *                        v                   v\n   223→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+--------+\n   224→     *                 | Not          |     | Error (6)    |\n   225→     *                 | Running (4)  |     +--------------+\n   226→     *                 +--------------+\n   227→     *\n   228→     *\n   229→     * </pre>\n   230→     * Note the following:\n   231→     * <ul>\n   232→     *   <li>\n   233→     *     RUNNING state will transit to REBALANCING if any of its threads is in PARTITION_REVOKED or PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED state\n   234→     *   </li>\n   235→     *   <li>\n   236→     *     REBALANCING state will transit to RUNNING if all of its threads are in RUNNING state\n   237→     *     (Note: a thread transits to RUNNING state, if all active tasks got restored are ready for processing.\n   238→     *     Standby tasks are not considered.)\n   239→     *   </li>\n   240→     *   <li>\n   241→     *     Any state except NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN (whenever close is called)\n   242→     *   </li>\n   243→     *   <li>\n   244→     *     PENDING_SHUTDOWN and PENDING_ERROR are transitory states where the Streams application gracefully closes\n   245→     *     its existing resources before transitioning into their corresponding terminal states. These states are\n   246→     *     not recoverable, and only a restart would get an application back to the RUNNING state.\n   247→     *   </li>\n   248→     *   <li>\n   249→     *     Of special importance: If the global stream thread dies, or all stream threads die (or both) then\n   250→     *     the instance will be in the ERROR state. The user will not need to close it.\n   251→     *   </li>\n   252→     * </ul>\n   253→     */\n   254→    public enum State {\n   255→        // Note: if you add a new state, check the below methods and how they are used within Streams to see if\n   256→        // any of them should be updated to include the new state. For example a new shutdown path or terminal\n   257→        // state would likely need to be included in methods like isShuttingDown(), hasCompletedShutdown(), etc.\n   258→        CREATED(1, 3),          // 0\n   259→        REBALANCING(2, 3, 5),   // 1\n   260→        RUNNING(1, 2, 3, 5),    // 2\n   261→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(4),    // 3\n   262→        NOT_RUNNING,            // 4\n   263→        PENDING_ERROR(6),       // 5\n   264→        ERROR;                  // 6\n   265→\n   266→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   267→\n   268→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   269→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   270→        }\n   271→\n   272→        public boolean hasNotStarted() {\n   273→            return equals(CREATED);\n   274→        }\n   275→\n   276→        public boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n   277→            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(REBALANCING);\n   278→        }\n   279→\n   280→        public boolean isShuttingDown() {\n   281→            return equals(PENDING_SHUTDOWN) || equals(PENDING_ERROR);\n   282→        }\n   283→\n   284→        public boolean hasCompletedShutdown() {\n   285→            return equals(NOT_RUNNING) || equals(ERROR);\n   286→        }\n   287→\n   288→        public boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n   289→            return isShuttingDown() || hasCompletedShutdown();\n   290→        }\n   291→\n   292→        public boolean isValidTransition(final State newState) {\n   293→            return validTransitions.contains(newState.ordinal());\n   294→        }\n   295→    }\n   296→\n   297→    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n   298→    protected volatile State state = State.CREATED;\n   299→\n   300→    private boolean waitOnStates(final long waitMs, final State... targetStates) {\n   301→        final Set<State> targetStateSet = Set.of(targetStates);\n   302→        final long begin = time.milliseconds();\n   303→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   304→            boolean interrupted = false;\n   305→            long elapsedMs = 0L;\n   306→            try {\n   307→                while (!targetStateSet.contains(state)) {\n   308→                    if (waitMs > elapsedMs) {\n   309→                        final long remainingMs = waitMs - elapsedMs;\n   310→                        try {\n   311→                            stateLock.wait(remainingMs);\n   312→                        } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n   313→                            interrupted = true;\n   314→                        }\n   315→                    } else {\n   316→                        log.debug(\n   317→                            \"Cannot transit to {} within {}ms\",\n   318→                            Arrays.stream(targetStates).map(State::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(\" or \")),\n   319→                            waitMs\n   320→                        );\n   321→                        return false;\n   322→                    }\n   323→                    elapsedMs = time.milliseconds() - begin;\n   324→                }\n   325→            } finally {\n   326→                // Make sure to restore the interruption status before returning.\n   327→                // We do not always own the current thread that executes this method, i.e., we do not know the\n   328→                // interruption policy of the thread. The least we can do is restore the interruption status before\n   329→                // the current thread exits this method.\n   330→                if (interrupted) {\n   331→                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n   332→                }\n   333→            }\n   334→            return true;\n   335→        }\n   336→    }\n   337→\n   338→    /**\n   339→     * Sets the state\n   340→     * @param newState New state\n   341→     */\n   342→    private boolean setState(final State newState) {\n   343→        final State oldState;\n   344→\n   345→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   346→            oldState = state;\n   347→\n   348→            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState != State.NOT_RUNNING) {\n   349→                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, all other transitions than NOT_RUNNING (due to thread dying) will be\n   350→                // refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow appropriate error handling\n   351→                return false;\n   352→            } else if (state == State.NOT_RUNNING && (newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN || newState == State.NOT_RUNNING)) {\n   353→                // when the state is already in NOT_RUNNING, its transition to PENDING_SHUTDOWN or NOT_RUNNING (due to consecutive close calls)\n   354→                // will be refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow idempotent close calls\n   355→                return false;\n   356→            } else if (state == State.REBALANCING && newState == State.REBALANCING) {\n   357→                // when the state is already in REBALANCING, it should not transit to REBALANCING again\n   358→                return false;\n   359→            } else if (state == State.ERROR && (newState == State.PENDING_ERROR || newState == State.ERROR || newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN)) {\n   360→                // when the state is already in ERROR, its transition attempts to PENDING_SHUTDOWN, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR will\n   361→                // not throw an exception.\n   362→                return false;\n   363→            } else if (state == State.PENDING_ERROR && newState != State.ERROR) {\n   364→                // when the state is already in PENDING_ERROR, all other transitions than ERROR (due to thread dying) will be\n   365→                // refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow appropriate error handling\n   366→                return false;\n   367→            } else if (!state.isValidTransition(newState)) {\n   368→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Stream-client \" + clientId + \": Unexpected state transition from \" + oldState + \" to \" + newState);\n   369→            } else {\n   370→                log.info(\"State transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n   371→            }\n   372→            state = newState;\n   373→            stateLock.notifyAll();\n   374→\n   375→            // Ensure atomic state transition and notification\n   376→            if (stateListener != null) {\n   377→                stateListener.onChange(newState, oldState);\n   378→            }\n   379→        }\n   380→\n   381→        return true;\n   382→    }\n   383→\n   384→    /**\n   385→     * Return the current {@link State} of this {@code KafkaStreams} instance.\n   386→     *\n   387→     * @return the current state of this Kafka Streams instance\n   388→     */\n   389→    public State state() {\n   390→        return state;\n   391→    }\n   392→\n   393→    protected boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n   394→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   395→            return state.isRunningOrRebalancing();\n   396→        }\n   397→    }\n   398→\n   399→    protected boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n   400→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   401→            return state.hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown();\n   402→        }\n   403→    }\n   404→\n   405→    protected void validateIsRunningOrRebalancing() {\n   406→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   407→            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n   408→                throw new StreamsNotStartedException(\"KafkaStreams has not been started, you can retry after calling start()\");\n   409→            }\n   410→            if (!state.isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n   411→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"KafkaStreams is not running. State is \" + state + \".\");\n   412→            }\n   413→        }\n   414→    }\n   415→\n   416→    /**\n   417→     * Listen to {@link State} change events.\n   418→     */\n   419→    public interface StateListener {\n   420→\n   421→        /**\n   422→         * Called when state changes.\n   423→         *\n   424→         * @param newState new state\n   425→         * @param oldState previous state\n   426→         */\n   427→        void onChange(final State newState, final State oldState);\n   428→    }\n   429→\n   430→    /**\n   431→     * An app can set a single {@link KafkaStreams.StateListener} so that the app is notified when state changes.\n   432→     *\n   433→     * @param listener a new state listener\n   434→     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n   435→     */\n   436→    public void setStateListener(final KafkaStreams.StateListener listener) {\n   437→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   438→            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n   439→                stateListener = listener;\n   440→            } else {\n   441→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set StateListener before calling start(). Current state is: \" + state);\n   442→            }\n   443→        }\n   444→    }\n   445→\n   446→\n   447→    /**\n   448→     * Set the handler invoked when an internal {@link StreamsConfig#NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG stream thread}\n   449→     * throws an unexpected exception.\n   450→     * These might be exceptions indicating rare bugs in Kafka Streams, or they\n   451→     * might be exceptions thrown by your code, for example a NullPointerException thrown from your processor logic.\n   452→     * The handler will execute on the thread that produced the exception.\n   453→     * In order to get the thread that threw the exception, use {@code Thread.currentThread()}.\n   454→     * <p>\n   455→     * Note, this handler must be thread safe, since it will be shared among all threads, and invoked from any\n   456→     * thread that encounters such an exception.\n   457→     *\n   458→     * @param userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler the uncaught exception handler of type {@link StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler} for all internal threads\n   459→     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n   460→     * @throws NullPointerException if userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler is null.\n   461→     */\n   462→    public void setUncaughtExceptionHandler(final StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler) {\n   463→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   464→            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n   465→                Objects.requireNonNull(userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler);\n   466→                streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler =\n   467→                    (exception, skipThreadReplacement) ->\n   468→                        handleStreamsUncaughtException(exception, userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler, skipThreadReplacement);\n   469→                processStreamThread(thread -> globalStreamThread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler(\n   472→                        exception -> handleStreamsUncaughtException(exception, userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler, false)\n   473→                    );\n   474→                }\n   475→                processStreamThread(thread -> thread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler((t, e) -> { }\n   476→                ));\n   477→            } else {\n   478→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set UncaughtExceptionHandler before calling start(). \" +\n   479→                    \"Current state is: \" + state);\n   480→            }\n   481→        }\n   482→    }\n   483→\n   484→    private void replaceStreamThread(final Throwable throwable) {\n   485→        if (globalStreamThread != null && Thread.currentThread().getName().equals(globalStreamThread.getName())) {\n   486→            log.warn(\"The global thread cannot be replaced. Reverting to shutting down the client.\");\n   487→            log.error(\"Encountered the following exception during processing \" +\n   488→                    \" The streams client is going to shut down now. \", throwable);\n   489→            closeToError();\n   490→        }\n   491→        final StreamThread deadThread = (StreamThread) Thread.currentThread();\n   492→        deadThread.shutdown(org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation.REMAIN_IN_GROUP);\n   493→        addStreamThread();\n   494→        if (throwable instanceof RuntimeException) {\n   495→            throw (RuntimeException) throwable;\n   496→        } else if (throwable instanceof Error) {\n   497→            throw (Error) throwable;\n   498→        } else {\n   499→            throw new RuntimeException(\"Unexpected checked exception caught in the uncaught exception handler\", throwable);\n   500→        }"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01LKLuFxcBc5uWBtrG3P6Brt","session_id":"7476f412-4872-4f35-a0f3-27b26d2b1c70","uuid":"ad5edebb-a0ff-40c7-83e3-5d3d8d939b4b"}

StreamThread.State.DEAD);\n  1271→                    threads.remove(thread);\n  1272→                } else if (thread.state() == StreamThread.State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n  1273→                    log.debug(\"Skipping thread {} from num live threads computation since it's state is {}\",\n  1274→                              thread.getName(), StreamThread.State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN);\n  1275→                } else {\n  1276→                    numLiveThreads.incrementAndGet();\n  1277→                }\n  1278→            });\n  1279→            return numLiveThreads.get();\n  1280→        }\n  1281→    }\n  1282→\n  1283→    private int nextThreadIndex() {\n  1284→        final HashSet<String> allLiveThreadNames = new HashSet<>();\n  1285→        final AtomicInteger maxThreadId = new AtomicInteger(1);\n  1286→        synchronized (threads) {\n  1287→            processStreamThread(thread -> {\n  1288→                // trim any DEAD threads from the list, so we can reuse the thread.id\n  1289→                // this is only safe to do once the thread has fully completed shutdown\n  1290→                if (thread.state() == StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n  1291→                    threads.remove(thread);\n  1292→                } else {\n  1293→                    allLiveThreadNames.add(thread.getName());\n  1294→                    // Assume threads are always named with the \"-StreamThread-<threadId>\" suffix\n  1295→                    final int threadId = Integer.parseInt(thread.getName().substring(thread.getName().lastIndexOf(\"-\") + 1));\n  1296→                    if (threadId > maxThreadId.get()) {\n  1297→                        maxThreadId.set(threadId);\n  1298→                    }\n  1299→                }\n  1300→            });\n  1301→\n  1302→            final String baseName = clientId + \"-StreamThread-\";\n  1303→            for (int i = 1; i <= maxThreadId.get(); i++) {\n  1304→                final String name = baseName + i;\n  1305→                if (!allLiveThreadNames.contains(name)) {\n  1306→                    return i;\n  1307→                }\n  1308→            }\n  1309→            // It's safe to use threads.size() rather than getNumLiveStreamThreads() to infer the number of threads\n  1310→            // here since we trimmed any DEAD threads earlier in this method while holding the lock\n  1311→            return threads.size() + 1;\n  1312→        }\n  1313→    }\n  1314→\n  1315→    private long cacheSizePerThread(final int numStreamThreads) {\n  1316→        if (numStreamThreads == 0) {\n  1317→        globalStreamThread.resize(cacheSizePerThread);\n  1326→        }\n  1327→    }\n  1328→\n  1329→    private ScheduledExecutorService setupStateDirCleaner() {\n  1330→        return   public Cancellable schedule(final PunctuationSchedule sched) {\n    30→        synchronized (pq) {\n    31→            pq.add(sched);\n    32→        }\n    33→        return sched.cancellable();\n    34→    }\n    35→\n    36→    public void close() {\n    37→        synchronized (pq) {\n    38→            pq.clear();\n    39→        }\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    /**\n    43→     * ProcessorNodePunctuator processorNodePunctuator) {\n    46→        synchronized (pq) {\n    47→            boolean punctuated = false;\n    48→            PunctuationSchedule top = pq.peek();\n    49→            while (top != null && top.timestamp <= timestamp) {\n    50→                final PunctuationSchedule sched = top;\n    51→                pq.poll();\n    52→\n    53→                if 115→\n   116→    synchronized void flush() {\n   117→        flush(null);\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    private void flush(final LRUNode evicted) {\n   121→        numFlushes++;\n   122→\n   123→        if (log.isTraceEnabled()) {\n   124→            log.trace(\"Named cache {} stats on flush: #hits={}, #misses={}, #overwrites={}, #flushes={}\",\n   125→                name, hits(), misses(), overwrites(), flushes());\n   126→        }\n   127→\n   128→        if (listener == null) {\n   129→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"No listener for namespace \" + name + \" registered with cache\");\n   130→        }\n   131→\n   132→        if (dirtyKeys.isEmpty()) {\n   133→            return;\n   134→        }\n   135→\n   136→        final it to the list of\n   140→        // flushed entries and remove from dirtyKeys.\n   141→        if (evicted != null) {\n   142→            entries.add(new ThreadCache.DirtyEntry(evicted.key, evicted.entry.value(), evicted.entry));\n   143→            dirtyKeys.remove(evicted.key);\n   144→        }\n   145→\n   146→        for (final Bytes key : dirtyKeys) {\n   147→            final 150→            }\n   151→            entries.add(new ThreadCache.DirtyEntry(key, node.entry.value(), node.entry));\n   152→            node.entry.markClean();\n   153→            if (node.entry.value() == null) {\n   154→                deleted.add(node.key);\n   155→            }\n   156→        }\n   157→        // clear dirtyKeys before the listener is applied as it may be }\n   164→\n   165→    synchronized void put(final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n   166→        if (!value.isDirty() && dirtyKeys.contains(key)) {\n   167→            throw new the same key\",\n   170→                    key, name\n   171→                )\n   172→            );\n   173→        }\n   174→        LRUNode node = cache.get(key);\n   175→        if (node !=           hitRatioSensor.record((double) numReadHits / (double) (numReadHits + numReadMisses));\n   209→        }\n   210→        return node;\n   211→    }\n   212→\n   213→    private void (node.previous != null) {\n   221→            node.previous.next = node.next;\n   222→        } else {\n   223→            head = node.next;\n   224→        }\n   225→        if (node.next != null) putHead(final LRUNode node) {\n   233→        node.next = head;\n   234→        node.previous = null;\n   235→        if (head != null) {\n   236→            head.previous = node;\n   237→        }\n   238→        head = node;\n   239→        if (tail == null) {\n   240→            tail = head;\n   241→        }\n   242→    }\n   243→\n   244→    synchronized void evict() {\n   245→        if (tail == null) {\n   246→            return;\n   247→        }\n   248→        final LRUNode eldest = tail;\n   249→        currentSizeBytes -= eldest.size();\n   250→        remove(eldest);\n   251→ handleRuntimeException(anyOtherException);\n   167→            } finally {\n   168→                clearInputQueue();\n   169→                clearUpdatingAndPausedTasks();\n   170→                updaterMetrics.clear();\n   171→                log.info(\"State updater thread stopped\");\n   172→            }\n   173→        }\n   174→\n   175→        private void clearInputQueue() {\n   176→            tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n   177→            try {\n   178→                tasksAndActions.clear();\n   179→            } finally {\n   180→                tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n   181→            }\n   182→        }\n   183→\n   184→        // In each iteration:\n   185→        //   1) check if updating tasks need to be paused\n   186→        //   2) check if paused tasks need to be resumed\n   187→        //   3) restore those updating tasks\n   188→        //   4) checkpoint those updating task states\n   189→        //   5) idle waiting if there is no more tasks to be restored\n   190→        //\n   191→        //   Note that, 1-3) are measured as restoring time, while 4) and 5) measured separately\n   192→        //   as checkpointing time and idle time\n   193→        private void runOnce() {\n   194→            final long totalStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   195→            performActionsOnTasks();\n   196→\n   197→            resumeTasks();\n   198→            pauseTasks();\n   199→            restoreTasks(totalStartTimeMs);\n   200→\n   201→            maybeGetClientInstanceIds();\n   202→\n   203→            final long checkpointStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   204→            maybeCheckpointTasks(checkpointStartTimeMs);\n   205→\n   206→            final long waitStartTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   207→            waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead();\n   208→\n   209→            final long endTimeMs = time.milliseconds();\n   210→            final long totalWaitTime = Math.max(0L, endTimeMs - waitStartTimeMs);\n   211→            final long totalTime = Math.max(0L, endTimeMs - totalStartTimeMs);\n   212→\n   213→            recordMetrics(endTimeMs, totalTime, totalWaitTime);\n   214→        }\n   215→\n   216→        private void performActionsOnTasks() {\n   217→            tasksAndActionsLock.lock();\n   218→            try {\n   219→                for (final TaskAndAction taskAndAction : tasksAndActions()) {\n   220→                    final Action action = taskAndAction.action();\n   221→                    switch (action) {\n   222→                        case ADD:\n   223→                            addTask(taskAndAction.task());\n   224→                            break;\n   225→                        case REMOVE:\n   226→                            removeTask(taskAndAction.taskId(), taskAndAction.futureForRemove(), taskAndAction.suspendReason());\n   227→                            break;\n   228→                        default:\n   229→                            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Unknown action type \" + action);\n   230→                    }\n   231→                }\n   232→            } finally {\n   233→                tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n   234→            }\n   235→        }\n   236→\n   237→        private void resumeTasks() {\n   238→            if (isTopologyResumed.compareAndSet(true, false)) {\n   239→                for (final Task task : pausedTasks.values()) {\n   240→                    if (!topologyMetadata.isPaused(task.id().topologyName())) {\n   241→                        resumeTask(task);\n   242→                    }\n   243→                }\n   244→            }\n   245→        }\n   246→\n   247→        private void pauseTasks() {\n   248→            for (final Task task : updatingTasks.values()) {\n   249→                if (topologyMetadata.isPaused(task.id().topologyName())) {\n   250→                    pauseTask(task);\n   251→                }\n   252→            }\n   253→        }\n   254→\n   255→        private void restoreTasks(final long now) {\n   256→            try {\n   257→                final long restored = changelogReader.restore(updatingTasks);\n   258→                updaterMetrics.restoreSensor.record(restored, now);\n   259→            } catch (final TaskCorruptedException taskCorruptedException) {\n   260→                handleTaskCorruptedException(taskCorruptedException);\n   261→            } catch (final StreamsException streamsException) {\n   262→                handleStreamsException(streamsException);\n   263→            }\n   264→            final Set<TopicPartition> completedChangelogs = changelogReader.completedChangelogs();\n   265→            final List<Task> activeTasks = updatingTasks.values().stream().filter(Task::isActive).collect(Collectors.toList());\n   266→            for (final Task task : activeTasks) {\n   267→                maybeCompleteRestoration((StreamTask) task, completedChangelogs);\n   268→            }\n   269→        }\n   270→\n   271→        private void maybeGetClientInstanceIds() {\n   272→            if (fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId != -1) {\n   273→                if (!clientInstanceIdFuture.isDone()) {\n   274→                    if (fetchDeadlineClientInstanceId >= time.milliseconds()) {\n   275→                        try {\n   276→                            // if the state-updated thread has active work:\n   277→                            //    we pass in a timeout of zero into each `clientInstanceId()` call\n   278→                            //    to just trigger the \"get instance id\" background RPC;\n   279→                            //    we don't want to block the state updater thread that can do useful work in the meantime\n   280→                            // otherwise, we pass in 100ms to avoid busy waiting\n   281→                            clientInstanceIdFuture.complete(\n   282→                                restoreConsumer.clientInstanceId(\n   283→                                    allWorkDone() ? 327→\n   328→    /**\n   329→     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   330→     */\n   331→    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n   332→                                                final String topologyName) {\n   333→        try {\n   334→            lock();\n   335→            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   336→ removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   341→            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n   342→            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n   359→    }\n   360→\n   361→    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n   362→        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n   363→        builder.buildTopology();\n   364→\n   365→        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n   366→\n   367→        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n   368→        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n   369→        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n   370→        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n   371→\n   372→        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n   373→        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n   374→\n   375→        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n   376→        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   377→\n   378→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   379→            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   380→            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   381→            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n   382→                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n   383→            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n   384→        }\n   385→\n   386→        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n   387→        if (globalTopology != null) {\n   388→            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n   389→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   390→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   391→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n   392→            } else {\n   393→                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n   394→                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n   395→            }\n   396→        }\n   397→        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   398→    }\n   399→\n   400→    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n   401→        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n   402→\n   403→        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   404→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   405→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   406→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n   407→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   408→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n   409→            }\n   410→        } else {\n   411→            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   412→            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n   413→                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n   414→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   415→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n   416→            }\n   417→        }\n   418→\n   419→        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n   420→        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   421→            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n   422→
