Let me create the final analysis:\n\n## Thorough Concurrency Analysis of Kafka Streams Codebase\n\nBased on my comprehensive exploration of the Kafka Streams codebase, I have identified multiple critical concurrency issues that pose risk of deadlocks and lost wakeups. Here are my findings:\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #1: Nested Lock Ordering - DefaultStateUpdater (HIGH RISK DEADLOCK)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Issue:** Nested lock acquisition in different orders across methods creates deadlock potential.\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Three ReentrantLocks used:\n  - Line 825: `private final Lock tasksAndActionsLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n  - Line 828: `private final Lock restoredActiveTasksLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n  - Line 830: `private final Lock exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n\n**Dangerous Code Pattern (Lines 1088-1099):**\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();           // LOCK 1\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();       // LOCK 2\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock();  // LOCK 3\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();  // UNLOCK in reverse\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Condition Variables:** Line 826 and 829\n- `tasksAndActionsCondition.await()` at line 450\n- `tasksAndActionsCondition.signalAll()` at lines 324, 697, 888, 911, 933, 945\n- `restoredActiveTasksCondition.await(deadline - now, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)` at line 966\n- `restoredActiveTasksCondition.signalAll()` at lines 715, 717\n\n**Hazards Identified:**\n1. Methods like `removeRestoredTask()` (line 591) acquire only `restoredActiveTasksLock`\n2. Methods like `removeFailedTask()` (line 611) acquire only `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n3. But `executeWithQueuesLocked()` acquires all three in order: tasksAndActions → restoredActiveTasks → exceptionsAndFailedTasks\n4. If thread A holds `restoredActiveTasksLock` and waits for `tasksAndActionsLock`, while thread B acquires locks in the order defined in `executeWithQueuesLocked()`, deadlock occurs\n5. **Lost Wakeup Risk:** Condition variables are signaled while holding locks, but in `waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead()` (line 445-459), the method acquires `tasksAndActionsLock`, calls `await()`, then releases. If signal happens between checking `allWorkDone()` and entering `await()`, the thread hangs indefinitely.\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #2: Multiple Monitor Locks with Inconsistent Ordering - KafkaStreams\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 192: `private final Object changeThreadCount = new Object();`\n- Line 297: `private final Object stateLock = new Object();`\n- Line 172: `protected final List<StreamThread> threads;` (implicitly synchronized access)\n\n**Problematic Method - addStreamThread() (Lines 1115-1148):**\n```java\npublic Optional<String> addStreamThread() {\n    if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n        final StreamThread streamThread;\n        synchronized (changeThreadCount) {    // LOCK 1\n            // ... operations ...\n            streamThread = createAndAddStreamThread(cacheSizePerThread, threadIdx);\n        }\n        synchronized (stateLock) {            // LOCK 2\n            if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n                streamThread.start();\n                return Optional.of(streamThread.getName());\n            }\n            // ...\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problematic Method - removeStreamThread() (Lines 1190-1234):**\n```java\nprivate Optional<String> removeStreamThread(final long timeoutMs) {\n    if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n        synchronized (changeThreadCount) {   // LOCK 1\n            for (final StreamThread streamThread : new ArrayList<>(threads)) {\n                // ... operations that may check state via stateLock indirectly ...\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Lock ordering inconsistency between `changeThreadCount` and `stateLock`:\n- In `addStreamThread()`: changeThreadCount acquired first, then stateLock\n- Other state checking methods may acquire stateLock independently\n- Cross-thread dependencies could cause A-B-B-A deadlock pattern\n\n**Additional Issue at Line 1979:**\n```java\nsynchronized (thread.getStateLock()) {\n    // External lock acquisition on thread's lock\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #3: TopologyMetadata ReentrantLock with Condition Variable Coordination\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Lines 88-89: ReentrantLock and Condition variable\n  ```java\n  public ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n  public Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n  ```\n\n**Usage Pattern (Lines 263-279):**\n```java\npublic void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n    if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n        try {\n            lock();  // Line 266\n            while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                try {\n                    version.topologyCV.await();  // Line 270 - POTENTIAL HANG\n                } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                    log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();  // Line 276\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazards:**\n1. **Spurious Wakeup Vulnerability:** The `while` loop checks `isEmpty()`, but the condition is checked BEFORE awaiting, creating a time-of-check vs time-of-use (TOCTOU) race\n2. **Missing Signaling Analysis:** If topology changes occur between isEmpty() check at line 264 and entering the lock at line 266, the await() at line 270 may never be woken\n3. **InterruptedException Swallowed:** Caught at line 271 but doesn't re-check condition - continues looping indefinitely if interrupted during topology wait\n\n**Signal Methods (Lines 220-227):**\n```java\npublic void wakeupThreads() {\n    try {\n        lock();\n        version.topologyCV.signalAll();  // Signals all waiting threads\n    } finally {\n        unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #4: ThreadCache Nested Synchronization\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/ThreadCache.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Method-level synchronization on ThreadCache (line 80: `resize()`)\n- Nested synchronization on individual NamedCache objects (line 91): `synchronized (cache)`\n\n**Code (Lines 80-101):**\n```java\npublic synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    final boolean shrink = newCacheSizeBytes < maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    maxCacheSizeBytes = newCacheSizeBytes;\n    if (shrink) {\n        // ...\n        while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n            final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n            synchronized (cache) {  // NESTED LOCK\n                final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n                cache.evict();\n                sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n            }\n            numEvicts++;\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** While holding ThreadCache's intrinsic lock, code acquires individual NamedCache locks. If NamedCache operations try to acquire ThreadCache lock back, a deadlock occurs. Additionally, `put()` method (line 171) acquires NamedCache lock independently:\n\n```java\npublic void put(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n    final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n    synchronized (cache) {  // Direct lock without ThreadCache lock\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\nInconsistent lock ordering between resize and put operations.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #5: Tasks Registry with Multiple Synchronized Methods (Coarse-Grained Locking)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/Tasks.java`\n\n**Issue:** Class-level synchronization on multiple methods without clear lock ordering guarantees.\n\n**Methods (Lines 198-389):**\n- Line 198: `public synchronized void addActiveTask()`\n- Line 217: `public synchronized void addStandbyTask()`\n- Line 238: `public synchronized void removeTask()`\n- Line 261: `public synchronized void replaceStandbyWithActive()`\n- Multiple query methods acquiring the same lock\n\n**Hazard:** Coarse-grained synchronization across the entire Tasks object can cause contention bottlenecks. Multiple state transitions under single lock increases holding time, risking:\n1. Lock convoy effects\n2. Priority inversion with GC pauses\n3. Nested calls to synchronized methods on same object could mask issues\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #6: StreamThread State Lock and External Synchronization\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamThread.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 308: `public final Object stateLock;` (exposed for external synchronization)\n- Multiple synchronized blocks at lines 256, 294, 300, 1071\n\n**Code Pattern:**\n```java\nState setState(final State newState) {\n    final State oldState;\n    synchronized (stateLock) {\n        oldState = state;\n        // state transition logic\n        state = newState;\n        if (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n            updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());  // EXTERNAL CALL UNDER LOCK\n        }\n        stateLock.notifyAll();  // Line 283\n    }\n    if (stateListener != null) {\n        stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);  // CALLED OUTSIDE LOCK\n    }\n    return oldState;\n}\n```\n\n**Hazards:**\n1. Calling `updateThreadMetadata()` while holding lock could cause nested lock acquisition\n2. `stateListener.onChange()` called outside lock - potential race condition if listener modifies state\n3. `notifyAll()` usage without condition variable increases wakeup spuriousness\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #7: GlobalStreamThread CountDownLatch with Condition Variable Interaction\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/GlobalStreamThread.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 76: `private final CountDownLatch initializationLatch = new CountDownLatch(1);`\n- Line 134: `private final Object stateLock = new Object();`\n\n**Code (Lines 454-457):**\n```java\npublic synchronized void start() {\n    super.start();\n    try {\n        initializationLatch.await();  // Potentially blocks under synchronized\n    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Calling `await()` on CountDownLatch while method is synchronized can block other threads from calling synchronized methods. This is a known anti-pattern that can cause thread starvation.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #8: PunctuationQueue Synchronized on Collection\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/PunctuationQueue.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 27: `private final PriorityQueue<PunctuationSchedule> pq = new PriorityQueue<>();`\n- Synchronized blocks at lines 30, 37, 46, 74\n\n**Code (Lines 45-68):**\n```java\nboolean maybePunctuate(final long timestamp, final PunctuationType type, ...) {\n    synchronized (pq) {\n        boolean punctuated = false;\n        PunctuationSchedule top = pq.peek();\n        while (top != null && top.timestamp <= timestamp) {\n            final PunctuationSchedule sched = top;\n            pq.poll();\n            if (!sched.isCancelled()) {\n                processorNodePunctuator.punctuate(sched.node(), timestamp, type, sched.punctuator());  // EXTERNAL CALL\n                if (!sched.isCancelled()) {\n                    pq.add(sched.next(timestamp));\n                }\n                punctuated = true;\n            }\n            top = pq.peek();\n        }\n        return punctuated;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Calling `processorNodePunctuator.punctuate()` while holding lock on `pq` can lead to:\n1. Nested lock acquisition if punctuator accesses shared state protected by different locks\n2. Long-held locks while doing processing work\n3. Callback-induced reentrancy issues\n\n---\n\n### Summary of Concurrency Bugs Found:\n\n| Issue | Severity | Type | Files |\n|-------|----------|------|-------|\n| Nested ReentrantLock ordering with Let me create the final analysis:\n\n## Thorough Concurrency Analysis of Kafka Streams Codebase\n\nBased on my comprehensive exploration of the Kafka Streams codebase, I have identified multiple critical concurrency issues that pose risk of deadlocks and lost wakeups. Here are my findings:\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #1: Nested Lock Ordering - DefaultStateUpdater (HIGH RISK DEADLOCK)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Issue:** Nested lock acquisition in different orders across methods creates deadlock potential.\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Three ReentrantLocks used:\n  - Line 825: `private final Lock tasksAndActionsLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n  - Line 828: `private final Lock restoredActiveTasksLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n  - Line 830: `private final Lock exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock = new ReentrantLock();`\n\n**Dangerous Code Pattern (Lines 1088-1099):**\n```java\nprivate <T> Set<T> executeWithQueuesLocked(final Supplier<Set<T>> action) {\n    tasksAndActionsLock.lock();           // LOCK 1\n    restoredActiveTasksLock.lock();       // LOCK 2\n    exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.lock();  // LOCK 3\n    try {\n        return action.get();\n    } finally {\n        exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock.unlock();  // UNLOCK in reverse\n        restoredActiveTasksLock.unlock();\n        tasksAndActionsLock.unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Condition Variables:** Line 826 and 829\n- `tasksAndActionsCondition.await()` at line 450\n- `tasksAndActionsCondition.signalAll()` at lines 324, 697, 888, 911, 933, 945\n- `restoredActiveTasksCondition.await(deadline - now, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)` at line 966\n- `restoredActiveTasksCondition.signalAll()` at lines 715, 717\n\n**Hazards Identified:**\n1. Methods like `removeRestoredTask()` (line 591) acquire only `restoredActiveTasksLock`\n2. Methods like `removeFailedTask()` (line 611) acquire only `exceptionsAndFailedTasksLock`\n3. But `executeWithQueuesLocked()` acquires all three in order: tasksAndActions → restoredActiveTasks → exceptionsAndFailedTasks\n4. If thread A holds `restoredActiveTasksLock` and waits for `tasksAndActionsLock`, while thread B acquires locks in the order defined in `executeWithQueuesLocked()`, deadlock occurs\n5. **Lost Wakeup Risk:** Condition variables are signaled while holding locks, but in `waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead()` (line 445-459), the method acquires `tasksAndActionsLock`, calls `await()`, then releases. If signal happens between checking `allWorkDone()` and entering `await()`, the thread hangs indefinitely.\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #2: Multiple Monitor Locks with Inconsistent Ordering - KafkaStreams\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 192: `private final Object changeThreadCount = new Object();`\n- Line 297: `private final Object stateLock = new Object();`\n- Line 172: `protected final List<StreamThread> threads;` (implicitly synchronized access)\n\n**Problematic Method - addStreamThread() (Lines 1115-1148):**\n```java\npublic Optional<String> addStreamThread() {\n    if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n        final StreamThread streamThread;\n        synchronized (changeThreadCount) {    // LOCK 1\n            // ... operations ...\n            streamThread = createAndAddStreamThread(cacheSizePerThread, threadIdx);\n        }\n        synchronized (stateLock) {            // LOCK 2\n            if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n                streamThread.start();\n                return Optional.of(streamThread.getName());\n            }\n            // ...\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Problematic Method - removeStreamThread() (Lines 1190-1234):**\n```java\nprivate Optional<String> removeStreamThread(final long timeoutMs) {\n    if (isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n        synchronized (changeThreadCount) {   // LOCK 1\n            for (final StreamThread streamThread : new ArrayList<>(threads)) {\n                // ... operations that may check state via stateLock indirectly ...\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Lock ordering inconsistency between `changeThreadCount` and `stateLock`:\n- In `addStreamThread()`: changeThreadCount acquired first, then stateLock\n- Other state checking methods may acquire stateLock independently\n- Cross-thread dependencies could cause A-B-B-A deadlock pattern\n\n**Additional Issue at Line 1979:**\n```java\nsynchronized (thread.getStateLock()) {\n    // External lock acquisition on thread's lock\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL ISSUE #3: TopologyMetadata ReentrantLock with Condition Variable Coordination\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Lines 88-89: ReentrantLock and Condition variable\n  ```java\n  public ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n  public Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n  ```\n\n**Usage Pattern (Lines 263-279):**\n```java\npublic void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n    if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n        try {\n            lock();  // Line 266\n            while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                try {\n                    version.topologyCV.await();  // Line 270 - POTENTIAL HANG\n                } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                    log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();  // Line 276\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazards:**\n1. **Spurious Wakeup Vulnerability:** The `while` loop checks `isEmpty()`, but the condition is checked BEFORE awaiting, creating a time-of-check vs time-of-use (TOCTOU) race\n2. **Missing Signaling Analysis:** If topology changes occur between isEmpty() check at line 264 and entering the lock at line 266, the await() at line 270 may never be woken\n3. **InterruptedException Swallowed:** Caught at line 271 but doesn't re-check condition - continues looping indefinitely if interrupted during topology wait\n\n**Signal Methods (Lines 220-227):**\n```java\npublic void wakeupThreads() {\n    try {\n        lock();\n        version.topologyCV.signalAll();  // Signals all waiting threads\n    } finally {\n        unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #4: ThreadCache Nested Synchronization\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/ThreadCache.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Method-level synchronization on ThreadCache (line 80: `resize()`)\n- Nested synchronization on individual NamedCache objects (line 91): `synchronized (cache)`\n\n**Code (Lines 80-101):**\n```java\npublic synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    final boolean shrink = newCacheSizeBytes < maxCacheSizeBytes;\n    maxCacheSizeBytes = newCacheSizeBytes;\n    if (shrink) {\n        // ...\n        while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n            final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n            synchronized (cache) {  // NESTED LOCK\n                final long oldSize = cache.sizeInBytes();\n                cache.evict();\n                sizeInBytes.getAndAdd(cache.sizeInBytes() - oldSize);\n            }\n            numEvicts++;\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** While holding ThreadCache's intrinsic lock, code acquires individual NamedCache locks. If NamedCache operations try to acquire ThreadCache lock back, a deadlock occurs. Additionally, `put()` method (line 171) acquires NamedCache lock independently:\n\n```java\npublic void put(final String namespace, final Bytes key, final LRUCacheEntry value) {\n    final NamedCache cache = getOrCreateCache(namespace);\n    synchronized (cache) {  // Direct lock without ThreadCache lock\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\nInconsistent lock ordering between resize and put operations.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #5: Tasks Registry with Multiple Synchronized Methods (Coarse-Grained Locking)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/Tasks.java`\n\n**Issue:** Class-level synchronization on multiple methods without clear lock ordering guarantees.\n\n**Methods (Lines 198-389):**\n- Line 198: `public synchronized void addActiveTask()`\n- Line 217: `public synchronized void addStandbyTask()`\n- Line 238: `public synchronized void removeTask()`\n- Line 261: `public synchronized void replaceStandbyWithActive()`\n- Multiple query methods acquiring the same lock\n\n**Hazard:** Coarse-grained synchronization across the entire Tasks object can cause contention bottlenecks. Nested calls to synchronized methods on same object could mask issues\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #6: StreamThread State Lock and External Synchronization\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamThread.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 308: `public final Object stateLock;` (exposed for external synchronization)\n- Multiple synchronized blocks at lines 256, 294, 300, 1071\n\n**Code Pattern:**\n```java\nState setState(final State newState) {\n    final State oldState;\n    synchronized (stateLock) {\n        oldState = state;\n        // state transition logic\n        state = newState;\n        if (newState == State.RUNNING) {\n            updateThreadMetadata(taskManager.activeTaskMap(), taskManager.standbyTaskMap());  // EXTERNAL CALL UNDER LOCK\n        }\n        stateLock.notifyAll();  // Line 283\n    }\n    if (stateListener != null) {\n        stateListener.onChange(this, state, oldState);  // CALLED OUTSIDE LOCK\n    }\n    return oldState;\n}\n```\n\n**Hazards:**\n1. Calling `updateThreadMetadata()` while holding lock could cause nested lock acquisition\n2. `stateListener.onChange()` called outside lock - potential race condition if listener modifies state\n3. `notifyAll()` usage without condition variable increases wakeup spuriousness\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #7: GlobalStreamThread CountDownLatch with Condition Variable Interaction\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/GlobalStreamThread.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 76: `private final CountDownLatch initializationLatch = new CountDownLatch(1);`\n- Line 134: `private final Object stateLock = new Object();`\n\n**Code (Lines 454-457):**\n```java\npublic synchronized void start() {\n    super.start();\n    try {\n        initializationLatch.await();  // Potentially blocks under synchronized\n    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Calling `await()` on CountDownLatch while method is synchronized can block other threads from calling synchronized methods. This is a known anti-pattern that can cause thread starvation.\n\n---\n\n### ISSUE #8: PunctuationQueue Synchronized on Collection\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/PunctuationQueue.java`\n\n**Lock Details:**\n- Line 27: `private final PriorityQueue<PunctuationSchedule> pq = new PriorityQueue<>();`\n- Synchronized blocks at lines 30, 37, 46, 74\n\n**Code (Lines 45-68):**\n```java\nboolean maybePunctuate(final long timestamp, final PunctuationType type, ...) {\n    synchronized (pq) {\n        boolean punctuated = false;\n        PunctuationSchedule top = pq.peek();\n        while (top != null && top.timestamp <= timestamp) {\n            final PunctuationSchedule sched = top;\n            pq.poll();\n            if (!sched.isCancelled()) {\n                processorNodePunctuator.punctuate(sched.node(), timestamp, type, sched.punctuator());  // EXTERNAL CALL\n                if (!sched.isCancelled()) {\n                    pq.add(sched.next(timestamp));\n                }\n                punctuated = true;\n            }\n            top = pq.peek();\n        }\n        return punctuated;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Hazard:** Calling `processorNodePunctuator.punctuate()` while holding lock on `pq` can lead to:\n1. Nested lock acquisition if punctuator accesses shared state protected by different locks\n2. Long-held locks while doing processing work\n3. /**\n   196→     * Kafka Streams states are the possible state that a Kafka Streams instance can be in.\n   197→     * An instance must only be in one state at a time.\n   198→     * The expected state transition with the following defined states is:\n   199→     *\n   200→     * <pre>\n   201→     *                 +--------------+\n   202→     *         +&lt;----- | Created (0)  |\n   203→     *         |       +-----+--------+\n   204→     *         |             |\n   205→     *         |             v\n   206→     *         |       +----+--+------+\n   207→     *         |       | Re-    +----+-------+\n   218→     *         +-----&gt; | Pending      |     | Pending    |\n   219→     *                 | Shutdown (3) |     | Error (5)  |\n   220→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+------+\n   221→     *                        |                   |\n   222→     *                        v                   v\n   223→     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+--------+\n   224→     *                 | Not          |     | Error (6)    |\n   225→     *                 | Running (4)  |     +--------------+\n   226→     * of its threads are in RUNNING state\n   237→     *     (Note: a thread transits to RUNNING state, if all active tasks got restored are ready for processing.\n   238→     *     Standby tasks are not considered.)\n   239→     *   </li>\n   240→     *   <li>\n   241→     *     Any state except NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN (whenever close is called)\n   242→     *   </li>\n   243→     *   <li>\n   244→     *     PENDING_SHUTDOWN and PENDING_ERROR are transitory states where the Streams application gracefully closes\n   245→     *     its existing resources before transitioning into their corresponding terminal states. These states are\n   246→     *     not recoverable, and only a restart would get an application back to the RUNNING state.\n   247→     *   </li>\n   248→     *   <li>\n   249→     *     Of special importance: If the global stream thread dies, or all stream threads die (or both) then\n   250→     *     the instance will be in the ERROR state. The user will not need to close it.\n   251→     *   </li>\n   252→     * </ul>\n   253→     */\n   254→    public enum State {\n   255→        // Note: if you add a new state, check the below methods and how they are used within Streams to see if\n   256→        // any of them should be updated to include the new state.     // 0\n   259→        REBALANCING(2, 3, 5),   // 1\n   260→        RUNNING(1, 2, 3, 5),    // 2\n   261→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(4),    // 3\n   262→        NOT_RUNNING,            // 4\n   263→ possible state that a Kafka Streams instance can be in.\n     * An instance must only be in one state at a time.\n     * The expected state transition with the following defined states is:\n     *\n     * <pre>\n     *                 +--------------+\n     *         +&lt;----- | Created (0)  |\n     *         |       +-----+--------+\n     *         |             |\n     *         |             v\n     *         |       +----+--+------+\n     *         |       | Re-          |\n     *         +&lt;----- | Balancing (1)| --------&gt;+\n     *         |       +-----+-+------+          |\n     *         |             | ^                 |\n     *         |             v |                 |\n     *         |       +--------------+          v\n     *         |       | Running (2)  | --------&gt;+\n     *         |       +------+-------+          |\n     *         |              |                  |\n     *         |              v                  |\n     *         |       +------+-------+     +----+-------+\n     *         +-----&gt; | Pending      |     | Pending    |\n     *                 | Shutdown (3) |     | Error (5)  |\n     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+------+\n     *                        |                   |\n     *                        v                   v\n     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+--------+\n     *                 | Not          |     | Error (6)    |\n     *                 | Running (4)  |     +--------------+\n     *                 +--------------+\n     *\n     *\n     * </pre>\n     * Note the following:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>\n     *     RUNNING state will transit to REBALANCING if any of its threads is in PARTITION_REVOKED or PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED state\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     REBALANCING state will transit to RUNNING if all of its threads are in RUNNING state\n     *     (Note: a thread transits to RUNNING state, if all active tasks got restored are ready for processing.\n     *     Standby tasks are not considered.)\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     Any state except NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN (whenever close is called)\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     PENDING_SHUTDOWN and PENDING_ERROR are transitory states where the Streams application gracefully closes\n     *     its existing resources before transitioning into their corresponding terminal states. These states are\n     *     not recoverable, and only a restart would get an application back to the RUNNING state.\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     Of special importance: If the global stream thread dies, or all stream threads die (or both) then\n     *     the instance will be in the ERROR state. The user will not need to close it.\n     *   </li>\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    public enum State {\n        // Note: if you add a new state, check the below methods and how they are used within Streams to see if\n        // any of them should be updated to include the new state. For example a new shutdown path or terminal\n        // state would likely need to be included in methods like isShuttingDown(), hasCompletedShutdown(), etc.\n        CREATED(1, 3),          // 0\n        REBALANCING(2, 3, 5),   // 1\n        RUNNING(1, 2, 3, 5),    // 2\n        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(4),    // 3\n        NOT_RUNNING,            // 4\n        PENDING_ERROR(6),       // 5\n        ERROR;                  // 6\n\n        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n\n        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasNotStarted() {\n            return equals(CREATED);\n        }\n\n        public boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(REBALANCING);\n        }\n\n        public boolean isShuttingDown() {\n            return equals(PENDING_SHUTDOWN) || equals(PENDING_ERROR);\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasCompletedShutdown() {\n            return equals(NOT_RUNNING) || equals(ERROR);\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n            return isShuttingDown() || hasCompletedShutdown();\n        }\n\n        public boolean isValidTransition(final State newState) {\n            return validTransitions.contains(newState.ordinal());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n    protected volatile State state = State.CREATED;\n\n    private boolean waitOnStates(final long waitMs, final State... targetStates) {\n        final Set<State> targetStateSet = Set.of(targetStates);\n        final long begin = time.milliseconds();\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            boolean interrupted = false;\n            long elapsedMs = 0L;\n            try {\n                while (!targetStateSet.contains(state)) {\n                    if (waitMs > elapsedMs) {\n                        final long remainingMs = waitMs - elapsedMs;","numLines":130,"startLine":180,"totalLines":2154}}}

There's a race condition where Thread A checks if the topology is empty before acquiring the lock, then Thread B adds a topology and signals the condition variable, but Thread A hasn't called await yet. in a separate thread;\n        // save the current thread so that if it is a stream thread\n        // we don't attempt to join it and cause a deadlock\n        return new Thread(() -> {\n            // notify all the threads to stop; avoid deadlocks by stopping any\n            // further state reports from the thread since we're shutting down\n            int numStreamThreads = processStreamThread(\n                streamThread -> streamThread.shutdown(operation)\n            );\n\n            log.info(\"Shutting down {} stream threads\", numStreamThreads);\n\n            topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads();\n\n            numStreamThreads = processStreamThread(thread -> {\n                try {\n                    if (!thread.isRunning()) {\n                        log.debug(\"Shutdown {} complete\", thread.getName());\n\n                        thread.join();\n                    }\n                } catch (final InterruptedException ex) {\n                    log.warn(\"Shutdown {} interrupted\", thread.getName());\n\n                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                }\n            });\n\n            log.info(\"Shutdown {} stream threads complete\", numStreamThreads);\n\n            if (globalStreamThread != null) {\n                log.info(\"Shutting down the global stream threads\");\n\n                globalStreamThread.shutdown();\n            }\n\n            if (globalStreamThread != null && !globalStreamThread.stillRunning()) {\n                try {\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012HHyZe3F4nDBph6f8qC34u","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! My analysis is confirmed. The callback IS being made while holding stateLock (line 377 is still inside the synchronized block that started at line 345). Now let me verify that this is the critical issue by creating a final comprehensive report:\n\n## COMPREHENSIVE DEADLOCK AND HANG ANALYSIS - KAFKA STREAMS\n\nBased on thorough analysis of the Kafka Streams codebase at `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/`, I have identified **SPECIFIC DEADLOCK AND HANG BUGS**:\n\n---\n\n### **CRITICAL BUG #1: Lost Wakeup in maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java` lines 263-279\n\n**Lock Acquisition Chain:**\n```\nmaybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology():\n  lock() → topologyLock.lock() [ReentrantLock]\n  while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()):\n    version.topologyCV.await()  // Releases topologyLock, reacquires on return\n```\n\n**The Bug:**\n```java\nwhile (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n    try {\n        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is currently empty, waiting for something to process\");\n        version.topologyCV.await();\n    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n        // BUG: Exception is SWALLOWED - continues loop without checking if wakeup was lost\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Deadlock Scenario:**\n1. Thread A waits in `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()` → `await()`\n2. Thread B calls `registerAndBuildNewTopology()` → `wakeupThreads()` → `signalAll()`\n3. Thread A receives an InterruptedException (from shutdown, other interrupts)\n4. Exception is caught and logged, then the loop CONTINUES\n5. Loop condition is rechecked while holding topologyLock\n6. If topology is still empty, Thread A goes back to `await()`\n7. **The signalAll() from Thread B was LOST** - Thread A never wakes up naturally again\n8. If Thread B holds topologyLock trying to add a topology, and Thread A is waiting indefinitely, this can cause hangs\n\n**Lock Order:** `topologyLock` → `topologyCV`\n\n**Fix Needed:** Don't swallow InterruptedException or ensure spurious wakeup handling is correct\n\n---\n\n### **CRITICAL BUG #2: Callback Invoked While Holding stateLock**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java` lines 342-382\n\n**Lock Acquisition Chain:**\n```\nKafkaStreams.setState(newState):\n  synchronized(stateLock) {\n    state = newState\n    stateLock.notifyAll()\n    if (stateListener != null) {\n      stateListener.onChange(newState, oldState)  // CALLBACK UNDER LOCK\n    }\n  }\n```\n\n**The Problem - Callback is made WHILE HOLDING stateLock:**\n\nThe `onChange()` callback (lines 376-378) is invoked INSIDE the synchronized block, while `stateLock` is held.\n\n**Callback Implementation in StreamStateListener:**\n```java\n// Lines 649-676 in KafkaStreams.java\n@Override\npublic synchronized void onChange(final Thread thread,\n                                  final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractNewState,\n                                  final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractOldState) {\n    if (thread instanceof StreamThread) {\n        final StreamThread.State newState = (StreamThread.State) abstractNewState;\n        threadState.put(thread.getId(), newState);\n        \n        if (newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_REVOKED || \n            newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED) {\n            setState(State.REBALANCING);  // RECURSIVE CALL TO setState()\n        } else if (newState == StreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n            maybeSetRunning();  // Can call setState(RUNNING)\n        }\n    }\n    // ...\n    else if (newState == GlobalStreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n        if (state != State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n            closeToError();  // Can call setState(PENDING_ERROR)\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Issue:** \n- onChange() is synchronized on `StreamStateListener.this`\n- onChange() calls `setState()` which tries to acquire `stateLock` AGAIN\n- Since the SAME thread is re-acquiring, Java's reentrant monitors allow this\n- However, this creates complexity and potential for deadlock if state transitions occur during callback\n\n**Lock Order Analysis:**\n- Path 1: StreamThread.setState() → stateListener.onChange() → KafkaStreams.setState()\n  - Locks: `StreamThread.stateLock` → `StreamStateListener.this` → `KafkaStreams.stateLock`\n  \n- Path 2: Other code → KafkaStreams.setState()\n  - Locks: `KafkaStreams.stateLock`\n\n**Deadlock Scenario:**\nWhile the reentrant nature prevents immediate deadlock on the same thread, the callback under lock violates synchronization best practices and can cause:\n1. **Hidden deadlock** if third party code in stateListener takes other locks\n2. **Priority inversion** - callback cannot yield control\n3. **Unbounded hold time** - stateLock held while executing unpredictable callback code\n\n---\n\n### **BUG #3: Unsafe Thread Iteration in processStreamThread()**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java` lines 1833-1838\n\n```java\nprotected int processStreamThread(final Consumer<StreamThread> consumer) {\n    final List<StreamThread> copy = new ArrayList<>(threads);  // UNSAFE - threads is SynchronizedList\n    for (final StreamThread thread : copy) consumer.accept(thread);\n    return copy.size();\n}\n```\n\n**Problem:**\n- `threads` is a SynchronizedList, but creating a copy with `new ArrayList<>(threads)` is NOT thread-safe\n- While the snapshot approach avoids holding locks during iteration, the `threads` access itself is unsynchronized\n- This can see inconsistent state if another thread modifies `threads` during the copy\n\n**Affected Call Sites:**\n1. Line 1400: `processStreamThread(StreamThread::start)` in `start()` - called with KafkaStreams.this locked\n2. Line 1475-1477: `processStreamThread(shutdown)` in `shutdownHelper()` - in shutdown thread\n3. Line 1268: Inside `numLiveStreamThreads()` where `synchronized(threads)` is held\n4. Line 1287: Inside `nextThreadIndex()` where `synchronized(threads)` is held\n\n**Critical Inconsistency:**\n- In `numLiveStreamThreads()` (line 1268) and `nextThreadIndex()` (line 1287), the code explicitly holds `synchronized(threads)` before calling `processStreamThread()`\n- But in `start()` (line 1400) and `shutdownHelper()` (line 1475), there's NO lock on `threads`\n- This creates a race condition where the snapshot may be incomplete or stale\n\n---\n\n### **BUG #4: Complex Lock Ordering in addStreamThread()**\n\n**Location:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java` lines 1115-1148\n\n**Lock Acquisition Order:**\n```\naddStreamThread():\n  [NO LOCK]\n  → isRunningOrRebalancing()  [reads stateLock]\n  → synchronized(changeThreadCount) {  [LOCK 1]\n      → nextThreadIndex()  [acquires synchronized(threads)]  [LOCK 2]\n      → resizeThreadCache()\n      → createAndAddStreamThread()\n        → streamStateListener.registerStreamThread()  [LOCK 3: StreamStateListener.this]\n    }\n  → synchronized(stateLock) {  [LOCK 4]\n      → streamThread.start()\n      → threads.remove()\n    }\n```\n\n**Lock Order: changeThreadCount → threads → StreamStateListener.this → stateLock**\n\n**Deadlock Scenario:**\nIf another thread acquires locks in reverse order:\n1. This calls `handleStreamsUncaughtException` in `KafkaStreams` which can call `replaceStreamThread()` → `addStreamThread()`.\n\nNow, `addStreamThread()` acquires `changeThreadCount` and then `stateLock`. The key question: is the StreamThread holding any locks at this point? Looking at line 906, it's called from the catch block in `run()`, so the thread would NOT be holding any locks (exceptions would have unwound the stack).\n \nLet me shift focus to what looks like the clearest issue: the state listener callback that fires while holding `stateLock` in `KafkaStreams.setState()`. The problem is that the state transition and notification happen atomically under the lock, but then the listener callback is invoked while still holding that lock, which could create deadlock scenarios if the listener tries to acquire other locks or wait on resources. Now I'm tracing what happens when Thread B tries to acquire the same monitor while Thread A is still holding it along with the KafkaStreams lock—Thread B gets blocked waiting for the StreamsStateListener monitor, which creates a potential deadlock scenario if the user callback in Thread A's path tries to interact with Thread B's state. BLOCKED: Thread A holds StreamsStateListener monitor\n\nThis is just contention, not a deadlock. Thread B waits for Thread A to finish.\n\nLet me trace through this more carefully. What if the user's `stateListener.onChange()` callback—invoked from within `KafkaStreams.setState()` while holding `stateLock`—calls `waitOnStates()`? That method tries to acquire `stateLock` again and then calls `wait()` on it. Since `wait()` releases all monitor holds, Thread A would release `stateLock` entirely, allowing other threads to acquire it. But here's the problem: if the expected state transition never happens, Thread A hangs indefinitely waiting for a notification that may never come.\n\nI should step back and look for simpler deadlock patterns—cases where one code path acquires Lock A then Lock B, while another acquires them in reverse order. 163→        synchronized (stateLock) {\n   164→            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n   165→                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, its transition to itself\n   166→                // will be refused but we do not throw exception here\n   167→                return;\n   168→            } else if (state == The `() -> state` is a supplier that reads the StreamThread's `state` field. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01MBe9TxCLft2QXUSu87mUq9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"`isAlive()` returns true for RUNNING, STARTING, PARTITIONS_REVOKED, PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED. PENDING_SHUTDOWN returns false.\n\nSo when `shutdown()` is called and the state transitions to PENDING_SHUTDOWN:\n- `isAlive()` returns false\n- The while loop condition in `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology` becomes false\n- BUT the thread is stuck in `await()` and needs to be SIGNALED to wake up and re-check\n\nSo the bug is: when `shutdown()` changes the state to PENDING_SHUTDOWN, the `topologyCV` condition must be signaled to wake up the thread so it can re-check `threadState.get().isAlive()`.\n\n\nIn `KafkaStreams.shutdownHelper()`, `topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads()` is called after `shutdown()` to wake up waiting threads, but `removeStreamThread()` never calls `wakeupThreads()` — that's the hang. Let me trace through the `removeStreamThread()` code to confirm.\n\nAfter calling `shutdown()` on a stream thread, the code waits for it to reach DEAD state, but if the thread is stuck in an `await()` call, it won't progress and `waitOnThreadState()` will just timeout. The real issue is that `removeStreamThread()` can be called with `Long.MAX_VALUE` as the timeout, which means the hang could persist indefinitely.\n\nThe first bug is clear: `removeStreamThread()` never calls `topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads()` after shutting down a thread, so if the thread is blocked in `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()`, it'll hang forever.\n\nNow I'm investigating whether there's a second concurrency bug related to `KafkaStreams.setState()`. The stateListener callback gets invoked while holding `stateLock`, and I need to trace through what happens when `close()` runs concurrently with a StreamThread state change to see if there's a synchronization issue there. ires `stateLock` → calls `stateListener.onChange()` while holding `stateLock` → releases `stateLock`\n2. Creates shutdown thread, starts it\n3. `waitOnStates(NOT_RUNNING)` → acquires `stateLock` → calls `stateLock.wait()` (releases `stateLock` but still holds `this`)\n\nThread B (shutdown thread):\n1. `processStreamThread(shutdown)` - shuts down all threads\n2. `wakeupThreads()` - signals topologyCV\n3. `processStreamThread(join)` - waits for threads to finish\n4. Eventually calls `setState(NOT_RUNNING)` → acquires `stateLock` → sets state → `stateLock.notifyAll()` → calls `stateListener.onChange()` while holding `stateLock`\n\nThread A wakes up from `stateLock.wait()` and sees the state is NOT_RUNNING, so it returns. But here's the problem: if the user's `stateListener.onChange()` callback tries to call `close()`, that requires `synchronized(this)`, and Thread A is still holding `this`. Meanwhile, Thread B holds `stateLock` and is trying to acquire `this` through the callback, creating a classic deadlock where each thread is waiting for a lock the other holds.\n\nThe real issue is that `setState()` calls the user callback while still holding `stateLock`, and if that callback tries to interact with methods that need `this`, we get deadlock. Let me check if there's any internal code path that could trigger this without relying on user callbacks.\n\nExamining `setState()`... The real issue is that the callback is being invoked while holding the lock, which blocks all other threads trying to access state—including `waitOnStates()` called from `close()`, and any state transitions. If the callback is slow or tries to acquire the lock from another thread, it causes a deadlock. a bug. The fix is to move the `stateListener.onChange()` call OUTSIDE the `synchronized(stateLock)` block, matching the pattern used in `StreamThread.setState()`.\n\nLet me now also verify the `removeStreamThread` issue once more and then write the fixes.\n\nActually, there's another issue. In the current code, `stateListener.onChange()` is called inside `synchronized(stateLock)`. `StreamsStateListener.onChange()` (internal) calls `KafkaStreams.setState()` which acquires `stateLock`. But `StreamsStateListener` is set on StreamThread/GlobalStreamThread, NOT on KafkaStreams. The user-set `stateListener` on KafkaStreams is different.\n\nSo the real question is: can the user `stateListener.onChange()` callback cause a deadlock even without the user doing anything wrong?\n\nThe scenario is: `close()` holds the monitor lock on `this` and calls `setState()`, which holds `stateLock` while invoking the user callback. If that callback tries to acquire the monitor lock—say, by calling `waitOnStates()`—we'd have a deadlock. The shutdown thread doesn't hold `this`, so it's not the direct culprit, but if `close()` is already waiting in `waitOnStates()` and the shutdown thread's `setState()` callback tries to grab `this`, that creates the deadlock scenario.\n\nThe fix is to call the user callback outside the lock. This is the standard pattern and avoids the issue entirely.\n\nNow I'll write out the fixes and create tests to verify they work correctly.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":666,"cache_read_input_tokens":101608,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":666,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7476f412-4872-4f35-a0f3-27b26d2b1c70","uuid":"5461681b-1e06-4d2b-9dec-a0a7b3e05549"}

org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.api.Processor}s or by using the {@link StreamsBuilder} which provides the high-level DSL to define\n * transformations.\n * <p>\n * One {@code KafkaStreams} instance can contain one or more threads specified in the configs for the processing work.\n * <p>\n * A {@code KafkaStreams} instance can co-ordinate with any other instances with the same\n * {@link StreamsConfig#APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG application ID} (whether in the same process, on other processes on this\n * machine, or on remote machines) as a single (possibly distributed) stream processing application.\n * These instances will divide up the work based on the assignment of the input topic partitions so that all partitions\n * are being consumed.\n * If instances are added or fail, all (remaining) instances will rebalance the partition assignment among themselves\n * to balance processing load and ensure that all input topic partitions are processed.\n * <p>\n * Internally a {@code KafkaStreams} instance contains a normal {@link KafkaProducer} and {@link KafkaConsumer} instance\n * that is used for reading input and writing output.\n * <p>\n * A simple example might look like this:\n * <pre>{@code\n * Properties props = new Properties();\n * props.put(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG, \"my-stream-processing-application\");\n * props.put(StreamsConfig.BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS_CONFIG, \"localhost:9092\");\n * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_KEY_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_VALUE_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n *\n * StreamsBuilder builder = new StreamsBuilder();\n * builder.<String, String>stream(\"my-input-topic\").mapValues(value -> String.valueOf(value.length())).to(\"my-output-topic\");\n *\n * KafkaStreams streams = new KafkaStreams(builder.build(), props);\n * streams.start();\n * }</pre>\n *\n * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.StreamsBuilder\n * @see org.apache.kafka.streams.Topology\n */\npublic class KafkaStreams implements AutoCloseable {\n\n    private static final String JMX_PREFIX = \"kafka.streams\";\n\n    // processId is expected to be unique across JVMs and to be used\n    // in userData of the subscription request to allow assignor be aware\n    // of the co-location of stream thread's consumers. It is for internal\n    // usage only and should not be exposed to users at all.\n    private final Time time;\n    private final Logger log;\n    protected final String clientId;\n    private final Metrics metrics;\n    protected final StreamsConfig applicationConfigs;\n    protected final List<StreamThread> threads;\n    protected final StreamsMetadataState streamsMetadataState;\n    private final ScheduledExecutorService stateDirCleaner;\n    private final ScheduledExecutorService rocksDBMetricsRecordingService;\n    protected final Admin adminClient;\n    private final StreamsMetricsImpl streamsMetrics;\n    private final long totalCacheSize;\n    private final StreamStateListener streamStateListener;\n    private final DelegatingStateRestoreListener delegatingStateRestoreListener;\n    private final UUID processId;\n    private final KafkaClientSupplier clientSupplier;\n    protected final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n    private final QueryableStoreProvider queryableStoreProvider;\n    private final DelegatingStandbyUpdateListener delegatingStandbyUpdateListener;\n    private final LogContext logContext;\n\n    GlobalStreamThread globalStreamThread;\n    protected StateDirectory stateDirectory = null;\n    private KafkaStreams.StateListener stateListener;\n    private BiConsumer<Throwable, Boolean> streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler;\n    private final Object changeThreadCount = new Object();\n\n    // container states\n    /**\n     * Kafka Streams states are the possible state that a Kafka Streams instance can be in.\n     * An instance must only be in one state at a time.\n     * The expected state transition with the following defined states is:\n     *\n     * <pre>\n     *                 +--------------+\n     *         +&lt;----- | Created (0)  |\n     *         |       +-----+--------+\n     *         |             |\n     *         |             v\n     *         |       +----+--+------+\n     *         |       | Re-          |\n     *         +&lt;----- | Balancing (1)| --------&gt;+\n     *         |       +-----+-+------+          |\n     *         |             | ^                 |\n     *         |             v |                 |\n     *         |       +--------------+          v\n     *         |       | Running (2)  | --------&gt;+\n     *         |       +------+-------+          |\n     *         |              |                  |\n     *         |              v                  |\n     *         |       +------+-------+     +----+-------+\n     *         +-----&gt; | Pending      |     | Pending    |\n     *                 | Shutdown (3) |     | Error (5)  |\n     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+------+\n     *                        |                   |\n     *                        v                   v\n     *                 +------+-------+     +-----+--------+\n     *                 | Not          |     | Error (6)    |\n     *                 | Running (4)  |     +--------------+\n     *                 +--------------+\n     *\n     *\n     * </pre>\n     * Note the following:\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>\n     *     RUNNING state will transit to REBALANCING if any of its threads is in PARTITION_REVOKED or PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED state\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     REBALANCING state will transit to RUNNING if all of its threads are in RUNNING state\n     *     (Note: a thread transits to RUNNING state, if all active tasks got restored are ready for processing.\n     *     Standby tasks are not considered.)\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     Any state except NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN (whenever close is called)\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     PENDING_SHUTDOWN and PENDING_ERROR are transitory states where the Streams application gracefully closes\n     *     its existing resources before transitioning into their corresponding terminal states. These states are\n     *     not recoverable, and only a restart would get an application back to the RUNNING state.\n     *   </li>\n     *   <li>\n     *     Of special importance: If the global stream thread dies, or all stream threads die (or both) then\n     *     the instance will be in the ERROR state. The user will not need to close it.\n     *   </li>\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    public enum State {\n        // Note: if you add a new state, check the below methods and how they are used within Streams to see if\n        // any of them should be updated to include the new state. For example a new shutdown path or terminal\n        // state would likely need to be included in methods like isShuttingDown(), hasCompletedShutdown(), etc.\n        CREATED(1, 3),          // 0\n        REBALANCING(2, 3, 5),   // 1\n        RUNNING(1, 2, 3, 5),    // 2\n        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(4),    // 3\n        NOT_RUNNING,            // 4\n        PENDING_ERROR(6),       // 5\n        ERROR;                  // 6\n\n        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n\n        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasNotStarted() {\n            return equals(CREATED);\n        }\n\n        public boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(REBALANCING);\n        }\n\n        public boolean isShuttingDown() {\n            return equals(PENDING_SHUTDOWN) || equals(PENDING_ERROR);\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasCompletedShutdown() {\n            return equals(NOT_RUNNING) || equals(ERROR);\n        }\n\n        public boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n            return isShuttingDown() || hasCompletedShutdown();\n        }\n\n        public boolean isValidTransition(final State newState) {\n            return validTransitions.contains(newState.ordinal());\n        }\n    }\n\n    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n    protected volatile State state = State.CREATED;\n\n    private boolean waitOnStates(final long waitMs, final State... targetStates) {\n        final Set<State> targetStateSet = Set.of(targetStates);\n        final long begin = time.milliseconds();\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            boolean interrupted = false;\n            long elapsedMs = 0L;\n            try {\n                while (!targetStateSet.contains(state)) {\n                    if (waitMs > elapsedMs) {\n                        final long remainingMs = waitMs - elapsedMs;\n                        try {\n                            stateLock.wait(remainingMs);\n                        } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                            interrupted = true;\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        log.debug(\n                            \"Cannot transit to {} within {}ms\",\n                            Arrays.stream(targetStates).map(State::toString).collect(Collectors.joining(\" or \")),\n                            waitMs\n                        );\n                        return false;\n                    }\n                    elapsedMs = time.milliseconds() - begin;\n                }\n            } finally {\n                // Make sure to restore the interruption status before returning.\n                // We do not always own the current thread that executes this method, i.e., we do not know the\n                // interruption policy of the thread. The least we can do is restore the interruption status before\n                // the current thread exits this method.\n                if (interrupted) {\n                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                }\n            }\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Sets the state\n     * @param newState New state\n     */\n    private boolean setState(final State newState) {\n        final State oldState;\n\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            oldState = state;\n\n            if (state == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && newState != State.NOT_RUNNING) {\n                // when the state is already in PENDING_SHUTDOWN, all other transitions than NOT_RUNNING (due to thread dying) will be\n                // refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow appropriate error handling\n                return false;\n            } else if (state == State.NOT_RUNNING && (newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN || newState == State.NOT_RUNNING)) {\n                // when the state is already in NOT_RUNNING, its transition to PENDING_SHUTDOWN or NOT_RUNNING (due to consecutive close calls)\n                // will be refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow idempotent close calls\n                return false;\n            } else if (state == State.REBALANCING && newState == State.REBALANCING) {\n                // when the state is already in REBALANCING, it should not transit to REBALANCING again\n                return false;\n            } else if (state == State.ERROR && (newState == State.PENDING_ERROR || newState == State.ERROR || newState == State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN)) {\n                // when the state is already in ERROR, its transition attempts to PENDING_SHUTDOWN, PENDING_ERROR or ERROR will\n                // not throw an exception.\n                return false;\n            } else if (state == State.PENDING_ERROR && newState != State.ERROR) {\n                // when the state is already in PENDING_ERROR, all other transitions than ERROR (due to thread dying) will be\n                // refused, but we do not throw exception here, to allow appropriate error handling\n                return false;\n            } else if (!state.isValidTransition(newState)) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Stream-client \" + clientId + \": Unexpected state transition from \" + oldState + \" to \" + newState);\n            } else {\n                log.info(\"State transition from {} to {}\", oldState, newState);\n            }\n            state = newState;\n            stateLock.notifyAll();\n\n            // Ensure atomic state transition and notification\n            if (stateListener != null) {\n                stateListener.onChange(newState, oldState);\n            }\n        }\n\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Return the current {@link State} of this {@code KafkaStreams} instance.\n     *\n     * @return the current state of this Kafka Streams instance\n     */\n    public State state() {\n        return state;\n    }\n\n    protected boolean isRunningOrRebalancing() {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            return state.isRunningOrRebalancing();\n        }\n    }\n\n    protected boolean hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown() {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            return state.hasStartedOrFinishedShuttingDown();\n        }\n    }\n\n    protected void validateIsRunningOrRebalancing() {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n                throw new StreamsNotStartedException(\"KafkaStreams has not been started, you can retry after calling start()\");\n            }\n            if (!state.isRunningOrRebalancing()) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"KafkaStreams is not running. State is \" + state + \".\");\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Listen to {@link State} change events.\n     */\n    public interface StateListener {\n\n        /**\n         * Called when state changes.\n         *\n         * @param newState new state\n         * @param oldState previous state\n         */\n        void onChange(final State newState, final State oldState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * An app can set a single {@link KafkaStreams.StateListener} so that the app is notified when state changes.\n     *\n     * @param listener a new state listener\n     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n     */\n    public void setStateListener(final KafkaStreams.StateListener listener) {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n                stateListener = listener;\n            } else {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set StateListener before calling start(). Current state is: \" + state);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n\n    /**\n     * Set the handler invoked when an internal {@link StreamsConfig#NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG stream thread}\n     * throws an unexpected exception.\n     * These might be exceptions indicating rare bugs in Kafka Streams, or they\n     * might be exceptions thrown by your code, for example a NullPointerException thrown from your processor logic.\n     * The handler will execute on the thread that produced the exception.\n     * In order to get the thread that threw the exception, use {@code Thread.currentThread()}.\n     * <p>\n     * Note, this handler must be thread safe, since it will be shared among all threads, and invoked from any\n     * thread that encounters such an exception.\n     *\n     * @param userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler the uncaught exception handler of type {@link StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler} for all internal threads\n     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n     * @throws NullPointerException if userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler is null.\n     */\n    public void setUncaughtExceptionHandler(final StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler) {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n                Objects.requireNonNull(userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler);\n                streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler =\n                    (exception, skipThreadReplacement) ->\n                        handleStreamsUncaughtException(exception, userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler, skipThreadReplacement);\n                processStreamThread(thread -> thread.setStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler(streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler));\n                if (globalStreamThread != null) {\n                    globalStreamThread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler(\n                        exception -> handleStreamsUncaughtException(exception, userStreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler, false)\n                    );\n                }\n                processStreamThread(thread -> thread.setUncaughtExceptionHandler((t, e) -> { }\n                ));\n            } else {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set UncaughtExceptionHandler before calling start(). \" +\n                    \"Current state is: \" + state);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void replaceStreamThread(final Throwable throwable) {\n        if (globalStreamThread != null && Thread.currentThread().getName().equals(globalStreamThread.getName())) {\n            log.warn(\"The global thread cannot be replaced. Reverting to shutting down the client.\");\n            log.error(\"Encountered the following exception during processing \" +\n                    \" The streams client is going to shut down now. \", throwable);\n            closeToError();\n        }\n        final StreamThread deadThread = (StreamThread) Thread.currentThread();\n        deadThread.shutdown(org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation.REMAIN_IN_GROUP);\n        addStreamThread();\n        if (throwable instanceof RuntimeException) {\n            throw (RuntimeException) throwable;\n        } else if (throwable instanceof Error) {\n            throw (Error) throwable;\n        } else {\n            throw new RuntimeException(\"Unexpected checked exception caught in the uncaught exception handler\", throwable);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void handleStreamsUncaughtException(final Throwable throwable,\n                                                final StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler,\n                                                final boolean skipThreadReplacement) {\n        final StreamsUncaughtExceptionHandler.StreamThreadExceptionResponse action = streamsUncaughtExceptionHandler.handle(throwable);\n\n        switch (action) {\n            case REPLACE_THREAD:\n                if (!skipThreadReplacement) {\n                    log.error(\"Replacing thread in the streams uncaught exception handler\", throwable);\n                    replaceStreamThread(throwable);\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Skipping thread replacement for recoverable error\");\n                }\n                break;\n            case SHUTDOWN_CLIENT:\n                log.error(\n                    \"Encountered the following exception during processing and the registered exception handler \" +\n                        \"opted to {}. The streams client is going to shut down now.\",\n                    action,\n                    throwable\n                );\n                closeToError();\n                break;\n            case SHUTDOWN_APPLICATION:\n                if (numLiveStreamThreads() == 1) {\n                    log.warn(\"Attempt to shut down the application requires adding a thread to communicate the shutdown. No processing will be done on this thread\");\n                    addStreamThread();\n                }\n                if (throwable instanceof Error) {\n                    log.error(\"This option requires running threads to shut down the application.\" +\n                            \"but the uncaught exception was an Error, which means this runtime is no \" +\n                            \"longer in a well-defined state. Attempting to send the shutdown command anyway.\", throwable);\n                }\n                if (Thread.currentThread().equals(globalStreamThread) && numLiveStreamThreads() == 0) {\n                    log.error(\"Exception in global thread caused the application to attempt to shutdown.\" +\n                            \" This action will succeed only if there is at least one StreamThread running on this client.\" +\n                            \" Currently there are no running threads so will now close the client.\");\n                    closeToError();\n                    break;\n                }\n                processStreamThread(StreamThread::sendShutdownRequest);\n                log.error(\"Encountered the following exception during processing \" +\n                        \"and sent shutdown request for the entire application.\", throwable);\n                break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Set the listener which is triggered whenever a {@link StateStore} is being restored in order to resume\n     * processing.\n     *\n     * @param globalStateRestoreListener The listener triggered when {@link StateStore} is being restored.\n     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n     */\n    public void setGlobalStateRestoreListener(final StateRestoreListener globalStateRestoreListener) {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n                delegatingStateRestoreListener.setUserStateRestoreListener(globalStateRestoreListener);\n            } else {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set GlobalStateRestoreListener before calling start(). \" +\n                    \"Current state is: \" + state);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Set the listener which is triggered whenever a standby task is updated\n     *\n     * @param standbyListener The listener triggered when a standby task is updated.\n     * @throws IllegalStateException if this {@code KafkaStreams} instance has already been started.\n     */\n    public void setStandbyUpdateListener(final StandbyUpdateListener standbyListener) {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            if (state.hasNotStarted()) {\n                this.delegatingStandbyUpdateListener.setUserStandbyListener(standbyListener);\n            } else {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Can only set StandbyUpdateListener before calling start(). \" +\n                        \"Current state is: \" + state);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get read-only handle on global metrics registry, including streams client's own metrics plus\n     * its embedded producer, consumer and admin clients' metrics.\n     *\n     * @return Map of all metrics.\n     */\n    public Map<MetricName, ? extends Metric> metrics() {\n        final Map<MetricName, Metric> result = new LinkedHashMap<>();\n        // producer and consumer clients are per-thread\n        processStreamThread(thread -> {\n            result.putAll(thread.producerMetrics());\n            result.putAll(thread.consumerMetrics());\n            // admin client is shared, so we can actually move it\n            // to result.putAll(adminClient.metrics()).\n            // we did it intentionally just for flexibility.\n            result.putAll(thread.adminClientMetrics());\n        });\n        // global thread's consumer client\n        if (globalStreamThread != null) {\n            result.putAll(globalStreamThread.consumerMetrics());\n        }\n        // self streams metrics\n        result.putAll(metrics.metrics());\n        return Collections.unmodifiableMap(result);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Class that handles stream thread transitions\n     */\n    private final class StreamStateListener implements StreamThread.StateListener {\n        private final Map<Long, StreamThread.State> threadState;\n        private GlobalStreamThread.State globalThreadState;\n\n        StreamStateListener(final GlobalStreamThread.State globalThreadState) {\n            this.threadState = new HashMap<>();\n            this.globalThreadState = globalThreadState;\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * If all threads are up, including the global thread, set to RUNNING\n         */\n        private void maybeSetRunning() {\n            // state can be transferred to RUNNING if\n            // 1) all threads are either RUNNING or DEAD\n            // 2) thread is pending-shutdown and there are still other threads running\n            final boolean hasRunningThread = threadState.values().stream().anyMatch(s -> s == StreamThread.State.RUNNING);\n            for (final StreamThread.State state : threadState.values()) {\n                if (state == StreamThread.State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN && hasRunningThread) continue;\n                if (state != StreamThread.State.RUNNING && state != StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                    return;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // the global state thread is relevant only if it is started. There are cases\n            // when we don't have a global state thread at all, e.g., when we don't have global KTables\n            if (globalThreadState != null && globalThreadState != GlobalStreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            setState(State.RUNNING);\n        }\n\n\n        @Override\n        public synchronized void onChange(final Thread thread,\n                                          final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractNewState,\n                                          final ThreadStateTransitionValidator abstractOldState) {\n            // StreamThreads first\n            if (thread instanceof StreamThread) {\n                final StreamThread.State newState = (StreamThread.State) abstractNewState;\n                threadState.put(thread.getId(), newState);\n\n                if (newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_REVOKED || newState == StreamThread.State.PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED) {\n                    setState(State.REBALANCING);\n                } else if (newState == StreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                    maybeSetRunning();\n                }\n            } else if (thread instanceof GlobalStreamThread) {\n                // global stream thread has different invariants\n                final GlobalStreamThread.State newState = (GlobalStreamThread.State) abstractNewState;\n                globalThreadState = newState;\n\n                if (newState == GlobalStreamThread.State.RUNNING) {\n                    maybeSetRunning();\n                } else if (newState == GlobalStreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                    if (state != State.PENDING_SHUTDOWN) {\n                        log.error(\"Global thread has died. The streams application or client will now close to ERROR.\");\n                        closeToError();\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private synchronized void registerStreamThread(final StreamThread streamThread) {\n            threadState.put(streamThread.getId(), streamThread.state());\n        }\n    }\n\n    static final class DelegatingStateRestoreListener implements StateRestoreListener {\n        private StateRestoreListener userStateRestoreListener;\n\n        private void throwOnFatalException(final Exception fatalUserException,\n                                           final TopicPartition topicPartition,\n                                           final String storeName) {\n            throw new StreamsException(\n                    String.format(\"Fatal user code error in store restore listener for store %s, partition %s.\",\n                            storeName,\n                            topicPartition),\n                    fatalUserException);\n        }\n\n        void setUserStateRestoreListener(final StateRestoreListener userStateRestoreListener) {\n            this.userStateRestoreListener = userStateRestoreListener;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onRestoreStart(final TopicPartition topicPartition,\n                                   final String storeName,\n                                   final long startingOffset,\n                                   final long endingOffset) {\n            if (userStateRestoreListener != null) {\n                try {\n                    userStateRestoreListener.onRestoreStart(topicPartition, storeName, startingOffset, endingOffset);\n                } catch (final Exception fatalUserException) {\n                    throwOnFatalException(fatalUserException, topicPartition, storeName);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onBatchRestored(final TopicPartition topicPartition,\n                                    final String storeName,\n                                    final long batchEndOffset,\n                                    final long numRestored) {\n            if (userStateRestoreListener != null) {\n                try {\n                    userStateRestoreListener.onBatchRestored(topicPartition, storeName, batchEndOffset, numRestored);\n                } catch (final Exception fatalUserException) {\n                    throwOnFatalException(fatalUserException, topicPartition, storeName);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onRestoreEnd(final TopicPartition topicPartition, final String storeName, final long totalRestored) {\n            if (userStateRestoreListener != null) {\n                try {\n                    userStateRestoreListener.onRestoreEnd(topicPartition, storeName, totalRestored);\n                } catch (final Exception fatalUserException) {\n                    throwOnFatalException(fatalUserException, topicPartition, storeName);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n
