out.\n     * @throws StreamsException For any other error that might occur.\n     */\n    public synchronized ClientInstanceIds clientInstanceIds(final Duration timeout) {\n        if (timeout.isNegative()) {\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The timeout cannot be negative.\");\n        }\n        if (state().hasNotStarted()) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"KafkaStreams has not been started, you can retry after calling start().\");\n        }\n        if (state().isShuttingDown() || state.hasCompletedShutdown()) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"KafkaStreams has been stopped (\" + state + \").\");\n        }\n\n        final Timer remainingTime = time.timer(timeout.toMillis());\n        final ClientInstanceIdsImpl clientInstanceIds = new ClientInstanceIdsImpl();\n\n        // (1) fan-out calls to threads\n\n        // StreamThread for main/restore consumers and producer(s)\n        final Map<String, KafkaFuture<Uuid>> clientFutures = new HashMap<>();\n        synchronized (changeThreadCount) {\n            for (final StreamThread streamThread : threads) {\n                clientFutures.putAll(streamThread.clientInstanceIds(timeout));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // GlobalThread\n        KafkaFuture<Uuid> globalThreadFuture = null;\n        if (globalStreamThread != null) {\n            globalThreadFuture = globalStreamThread.globalConsumerInstanceId(timeout);\n        }\n\n        // (2) get admin client instance id in a blocking fashion, while Stream/GlobalThreads work in parallel\n        try {\n            clientInstanceIds.setAdminInstanceId(adminClient.clientInstanceId(timeout));\n            remainingTime.update(time.milliseconds());\n        } catch (final IllegalStateException telemetryDisabledError) {\n            // swallow\n            log.debug(\"Telemetry is disabled on the admin client.\");\n        } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n            throw timeoutException;\n        } catch (final Exception error) {\n            throw new StreamsException(\"Could not retrieve admin client instance id.\", error);\n        }\n\n        // (3) collect client instance ids from threads\n\n        // (3a) collect consumers and producer from StreamsThread\n        for (final Map.Entry<String, KafkaFuture<Uuid>> clientFuture : clientFutures.entrySet()) {\n            final Uuid instanceId = getOrThrowException(\n                clientFuture.getValue(),\n                remainingTime.remainingMs(),\n                () -> String.format(\n                    \"Could not retrieve consumer/producer instance id for %s.\",\n                    clientFuture.getKey()\n                )\n            );\n            remainingTime.update(time.milliseconds());\n\n            // could be `null` if telemetry is disabled on the consumer itself\n            if (instanceId != null) {\n                final String clientFutureKey = clientFuture.getKey();\n                if (clientFutureKey.toLowerCase(Locale.getDefault()).endsWith(\"-producer\")) {\n                    clientInstanceIds.addProducerInstanceId(\n                            clientFutureKey,\n                            instanceId\n                    );\n                } else {\n                    clientInstanceIds.addConsumerInstanceId(\n                            clientFutureKey,\n                            instanceId\n                    );\n                }\n            } else {\n                log.debug(String.format(\"Telemetry is disabled for %s.\", clientFuture.getKey()));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // (3b) collect from GlobalThread\n        if (globalThreadFuture != null) {\n            final Uuid instanceId = getOrThrowException(\n                globalThreadFuture,\n                remainingTime.remainingMs(),\n                () -> \"Could not retrieve global consumer client instance id.\"\n            );\n            remainingTime.update(time.milliseconds());\n\n            // could be `null` if telemetry is disabled on the client itself\n            if (instanceId != null) {\n                clientInstanceIds.addConsumerInstanceId(\n                    globalStreamThread.getName() + \"-global-consumer\",\n                    instanceId\n                );\n            } else {\n                log.debug(\"Telemetry is disabled for the global consumer.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        return clientInstanceIds;\n    }\n\n    private <T> T getOrThrowException(\n        final KafkaFuture<T> future,\n        final long timeoutMs,\n        final Supplier<String> errorMessage) {\n        final Throwable cause;\n\n        try {\n            return future.get(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);\n        } catch (final java.util.concurrent.TimeoutException timeout) {\n            throw new TimeoutException(errorMessage.get(), timeout);\n        } catch (final ExecutionException exception) {\n            cause = exception.getCause();\n            if (cause instanceof TimeoutException) {\n                throw (TimeoutException) cause;\n            }\n        } catch (final InterruptedException error) {\n            cause = error;\n        }\n\n        throw new StreamsException(errorMessage.get(), cause);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns runtime information about the local threads of this {@link KafkaStreams} instance.\n     *\n     * @return the set of {@link ThreadMetadata}.\n     */\n    public Set<ThreadMetadata> metadataForLocalThreads() {\n        final Set<ThreadMetadata> threadMetadata = new HashSet<>();\n        processStreamThread(thread -> {\n            synchronized (thread.getStateLock()) {\n                if (thread.state() != StreamThread.State.DEAD) {\n                    threadMetadata.add(thread.threadMetadata());\n                }\n            }\n        });\n        return threadMetadata;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@link LagInfo}, for all store partitions (active or standby) local to this Streams instance. Note that the\n     * values returned are just estimates and meant to be used for making soft decisions on whether the data in the store\n     * partition is fresh enough for querying.\n     *\n     * <p>Note: Each invocation of this method issues a call to the Kafka brokers. Thus, it's advisable to limit the frequency\n     * of invocation to once every few seconds.\n     *\n     * @return map of store names to another map of partition to {@link LagInfo}s\n     * @throws StreamsException if the admin client request throws exception\n     */\n    public Map<String, Map<Integer, LagInfo>> allLocalStorePartitionLags() {\n        final List<Task> allTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n        processStreamThread(thread -> allTasks.addAll(thread.readyOnlyAllTasks()));\n        return allLocalStorePartitionLags(allTasks);\n    }\n\n    protected Map<String, Map<Integer, LagInfo>> allLocalStorePartitionLags(final List<Task> tasksToCollectLagFor) {\n        final Map<String, Map<Integer, LagInfo>> localStorePartitionLags = new TreeMap<>();\n        final Collection<TopicPartition> allPartitions = new LinkedList<>();\n        final Map<TopicPartition, Long> allChangelogPositions = new HashMap<>();\n\n        // Obtain the current positions, of all the active-restoring and standby tasks\n        for (final Task task : tasksToCollectLagFor) {\n            allPartitions.addAll(task.changelogPartitions());\n            // Note that not all changelog partitions, will have positions; since some may not have started\n            allChangelogPositions.putAll(task.changelogOffsets());\n        }\n\n        log.debug(\"Current changelog positions: {}\", allChangelogPositions);\n        final Map<TopicPartition, ListOffsetsResultInfo> allEndOffsets;\n        allEndOffsets = fetchEndOffsets(allPartitions, adminClient);\n        log.debug(\"Current end offsets :{}\", allEndOffsets);\n\n        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, ListOffsetsResultInfo> entry : allEndOffsets.entrySet()) {\n            // Avoiding an extra admin API lookup by computing lags for not-yet-started restorations\n            // from zero instead of the real \"earliest offset\" for the changelog.\n            // This will yield the correct relative order of lagginess for the tasks in the cluster,\n            // but it is an over-estimate of how much work remains to restore the task from scratch.\n            final long earliestOffset = 0L;\n            final long changelogPosition = allChangelogPositions.getOrDefault(entry.getKey(), earliestOffset);\n            final long latestOffset = entry.getValue().offset();\n            final LagInfo lagInfo = new LagInfo(changelogPosition == Task.LATEST_OFFSET ? latestOffset : changelogPosition, latestOffset);\n            final String storeName = streamsMetadataState.storeForChangelogTopic(entry.getKey().topic());\n            localStorePartitionLags.computeIfAbsent(storeName, ignored -> new TreeMap<>())\n                .put(entry.getKey().partition(), lagInfo);\n        }\n\n        return Collections.unmodifiableMap(localStorePartitionLags);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Run an interactive query against a state store.\n     * <p>\n     * This method allows callers outside the Streams runtime to access the internal state of\n     * stateful processors. See <a href=\"https://kafka.apache.org/documentation/streams/developer-guide/interactive-queries.html\">IQ docs</a>\n     * for more information.\n     * <p>\n     * NOTICE: This functionality is {@link Evolving} and subject to change in minor versions.\n     * Once it is stabilized, this notice and the evolving annotation will be removed.\n     *\n     * @param <R> The result type specified by the query.\n     * @throws StreamsNotStartedException If Streams has not yet been started. Just call {@link\n     *                                    KafkaStreams#start()} and then retry this call.\n     * @throws StreamsStoppedException    If Streams is in a terminal state like PENDING_SHUTDOWN,\n     *                                    NOT_RUNNING, PENDING_ERROR, or ERROR. The caller should\n     *                                    discover a new instance to query.\n     * @throws UnknownStateStoreException If the specified store name does not exist in the\n     *                                    topology.\n     */\n    @Evolving\n    public <R> StateQueryResult<R> query(final StateQueryRequest<R> request) {\n        final String storeName = request.getStoreName();\n        if (!topologyMetadata.hasStore(storeName)) {\n            throw new UnknownStateStoreException(\n                \"Cannot get state store \"\n                    + storeName\n                    + \" because no such store is registered in the topology.\"\n            );\n        }\n        if (state().hasNotStarted()) {\n            throw new StreamsNotStartedException(\n                \"KafkaStreams has not been started, you can retry after calling start().\"\n            );\n        }\n        if (state().isShuttingDown() || state.hasCompletedShutdown()) {\n            throw new StreamsStoppedException(\n                \"KafkaStreams has been stopped (\" + state + \").\"\n                    + \" This instance can no longer serve queries.\"\n            );\n        }\n        final StateQueryResult<R> result = new StateQueryResult<>();\n\n        final Map<String, StateStore> globalStateStores = topologyMetadata.globalStateStores();\n        if (globalStateStores.containsKey(storeName)) {\n            // See KAFKA-13523\n            result.setGlobalResult(\n                QueryResult.forFailure(\n                    FailureReason.UNKNOWN_QUERY_TYPE,\n                    \"Global stores do not yet support the KafkaStreams#query API. Use KafkaStreams#store instead.\"\n                )\n            );\n        } else {\n            for (final StreamThread thread : threads) {\n                final Set<Task> tasks = thread.readyOnlyAllTasks();\n                for (final Task task : tasks) {\n\n                    final TaskId taskId = task.id();\n                    final int partition = taskId.partition();\n                    if (request.isAllPartitions() || request.getPartitions().contains(partition)) {\n                        final StateStore store = task.store(storeName);\n                        if (store != null) {\n                            final StreamThread.State state = thread.state();\n                            final boolean active = task.isActive();\n                            if (request.isRequireActive()\n                                && (state != StreamThread.State.RUNNING || !active)) {\n\n                                result.addResult(\n                                    partition,\n                                    QueryResult.forFailure(\n                                        FailureReason.NOT_ACTIVE,\n                                        \"Query requires a running active task,\"\n                                            + \" but partition was in state \"\n                                            + state + \" and was \"\n                                            + (active ? \"active\" : \"not active\") + \".\"\n                                    )\n                                );\n                            } else {\n                                final QueryResult<R> r = store.query(\n                                    request.getQuery(),\n                                    request.isRequireActive()\n                                        ? PositionBound.unbounded()\n                                        : request.getPositionBound(),\n                                    new QueryConfig(request.executionInfoEnabled())\n                                );\n                                result.addResult(partition, r);\n                            }\n\n\n                            // optimization: if we have handled all the requested partitions,\n                            // we can return right away.\n                            if (!request.isAllPartitions()\n                                && result.getPartitionResults().keySet().containsAll(request.getPartitions())) {\n                                return result;\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (!request.isAllPartitions()) {\n            for (final Integer partition : request.getPartitions()) {\n                if (!result.getPartitionResults().containsKey(partition)) {\n                    result.addResult(partition, QueryResult.forFailure(\n                        FailureReason.NOT_PRESENT,\n                        \"The requested partition was not present at the time of the query.\"\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        return result;\n    }\n\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1197,"oldLines":6,"newStart":1197,"newLines":7,"lines":["                     if (streamThread.isThreadAlive() && (callingThreadIsNotCurrentStreamThread || numLiveStreamThreads() == 1)) {","                         log.info(\"Removing StreamThread {}\", streamThread.getName());","                         streamThread.shutdown(org.apache.kafka.streams.CloseOptions.GroupMembershipOperation.LEAVE_GROUP);","+                        topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads();","                         if (callingThreadIsNotCurrentStreamThread) {","                             final long remainingTimeMs = timeoutMs - {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01436mK24FDF6bqG4vKvr4FQ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_019BidrC7qzi5mHQaBBTcJDe","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/streams/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/fray/SetStateDeadlockTest.java","content":"package org.apache.kafka.streams.fray;\n\n/**\n * Fray test for the setState() deadlock bug.\n *\n * Bug: KafkaStreams.setState() used to call stateListener.onChange() INSIDE\n * synchronized(stateLock). This creates a deadlock when:\n *   - Thread A (close caller): holds 'this' monitor → waits on stateLock\n *   - Thread B (shutdown thread): holds stateLock → callback tries to acquire 'this'\n *\n * This test reproduces the exact lock pattern. With the bug, Fray finds a deadlock.\n * With the fix (callback outside stateLock), no deadlock is possible.\n */\npublic class SetStateDeadlockTest {\n\n    private final Object stateLock = new Object();\n    private volatile int state = 0; // 0=CREATED, 1=RUNNING, 2=PENDING_SHUTDOWN, 3=NOT_RUNNING\n    private volatile Runnable stateListener = null;\n\n    /** Mimics KafkaStreams.setState() — fixed version calls listener outside lock */\n    private boolean setState(int newState) {\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            state = newState;\n            stateLock.notifyAll();\n        }\n        // Fixed: listener called OUTSIDE the lock\n        if (stateListener != null) {\n            stateListener.run();\n        }\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /** Mimics KafkaStreams.close() — synchronized on 'this' */\n    public synchronized boolean close() {\n        setState(2); // PENDING_SHUTDOWN\n\n        // Start \"shutdown thread\" that will eventually set NOT_RUNNING\n        Thread shutdownThread = new Thread(() -> {\n            setState(3); // NOT_RUNNING — listener runs outside stateLock\n        });\n        shutdownThread.start();\n\n        // Wait for NOT_RUNNING\n        synchronized (stateLock) {\n            while (state != 3) {\n                try {\n                    stateLock.wait();\n                } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                    Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                    return false;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    /** Mimics a synchronized method that could be called from user callback */\n    public synchronized String getInfo() {\n        return \"state=\" + state;\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {\n        SetStateDeadlockTest instance = new SetStateDeadlockTest();\n\n        // The user's state listener calls a synchronized method on the instance.\n        // With the bug (callback inside stateLock), this deadlocks.\n        // With the fix (callback outside stateLock), this is safe.\n        instance.stateListener = instance::getInfo;\n        instance.state = 1; // RUNNING\n\n        Thread closeThread = new Thread(instance::close);\n        closeThread.start();\n        closeThread.join();\n    }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":822,"cache_read_input_tokens":111316,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":822,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7476f412-4872-4f35-a0f3-27b26d2b1c70","uuid":"0018e5ee-026e-4731-8757-ba4cb4cdc5e9"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01WVDdxnTNW5W4iHNCPHuwgH","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_014U9yNEmdQ8cBnGuyqZ89Mh","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/streams/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/fray/RemoveThreadHangTest.java","content":"package org.apache.kafka.streams.fray;\n\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.Condition;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;\n\n/**\n * Fray test for the removeStreamThread() lost-wakeup hang.\n *\n * Bug: removeStreamThread() calls shutdown() on a StreamThread but does NOT call\n * topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads(). If the thread is blocked in\n * maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology() (awaiting on topologyCV), it never wakes up\n * to see its state changed to PENDING_SHUTDOWN, causing an indefinite hang.\n *\n * Fix: After calling shutdown(), also call wakeupThreads() to signal topologyCV.\n *\n * This test reproduces the exact pattern with the fix applied.\n */\npublic class RemoveThreadHangTest {\n\n    private final ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n    private final AtomicBoolean topologyEmpty = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private final AtomicBoolean threadAlive = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private volatile boolean threadExited = false;\n\n    /** Mimics TopologyMetadata.maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology() */\n    private void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology() {\n        if (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n            topologyLock.lock();\n            try {\n                while (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                        Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                        return;\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                topologyLock.unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Mimics TopologyMetadata.wakeupThreads() */\n    private void wakeupThreads() {\n        topologyLock.lock();\n        try {\n            topologyCV.signalAll();\n        } finally {\n            topologyLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Mimics StreamThread.shutdown() */\n    private void shutdownThread() {\n        threadAlive.set(false);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Mimics removeStreamThread() — fixed version calls wakeupThreads() after shutdown.\n     * Without the wakeupThreads() call, the worker thread hangs in await() forever.\n     */\n    private void removeStreamThread() {\n        shutdownThread();\n        wakeupThreads(); // Fix: signal the condition so waiting thread can re-check\n    }\n\n    /** Mimics the StreamThread's run loop waiting on empty topology */\n    private void workerThreadRun() {\n        maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology();\n        threadExited = true;\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {\n        RemoveThreadHangTest instance = new RemoveThreadHangTest();\n\n        // Worker thread blocks on empty topology\n        Thread worker = new Thread(instance::workerThreadRun);\n        worker.start();\n\n        // Another thread removes (shuts down) the worker\n        Thread remover = new Thread(instance::removeStreamThread);\n        remover.start();\n\n        remover.join();\n        worker.join();\n\n        if (!instance.threadExited) {\n            throw new AssertionError(\"Worker thread did not exit — lost wakeup!\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1119,"cache_read_input_tokens":112138,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1119,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7476f412-4872-4f35-a0f3-27b26d2b1c70","uuid":"d919a0c9-1fee-4e63-93ee-4f292d482c2c"}

org.apache.kafka.streams.fray;\n\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.Condition;\nimport java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;\n\n/**\n * Fray test demonstrating the removeStreamThread() lost-wakeup hang (BUGGY version).\n * This should be detected by Fray as a hang/deadlock.\n *\n * Bug: shutdown() changes threadAlive to false but does NOT signal topologyCV,\n * so the worker thread remains stuck in await() forever.\n */\npublic class RemoveThreadHangBuggyTest {\n\n    private final ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n    private final AtomicBoolean topologyEmpty = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private final AtomicBoolean threadAlive = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private volatile boolean threadExited = false;\n\n    private void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology() {\n        if (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n            topologyLock.lock();\n            try {\n                while (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                        Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                        return;\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                topologyLock.unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void shutdownThread() {\n        threadAlive.set(false);\n    }\n\n    /** BUGGY: no wakeupThreads() call after shutdown */\n    private void removeStreamThread() {\n        shutdownThread();\n        // BUG: missing wakeupThreads() — worker thread stays stuck in await()\n    }\n\n    private void demonstrating the removeStreamThread() lost-wakeup hang (BUGGY version).\n * This should be detected by Fray as a hang/deadlock.\n *\n * Bug: shutdown() changes threadAlive to false but does NOT signal topologyCV,\n * so the worker thread remains stuck in await() forever.\n */\npublic class RemoveThreadHangBuggyTest {\n\n    private final ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    private final Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n    private final AtomicBoolean topologyEmpty = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private final AtomicBoolean threadAlive = new AtomicBoolean(true);\n    private volatile boolean threadExited = false;\n\n    private void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology() {\n        if (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n            topologyLock.lock();\n            try {\n                while (topologyEmpty.get() && threadAlive.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (InterruptedException e) {\n                        Thread.currentThread().interrupt();\n                        return;\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                topologyLock.unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void shutdownThread() {\n        threadAlive.set(false);\n    }\n\n    /** BUGGY: no wakeupThreads() call after shutdown */\n    private void removeStreamThread() {\n        shutdownThread();\n        // BUG: missing wakeupThreads() — worker thread stays stuck in await()\n    }\n\n    private void workerThreadRun() {\n        maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology();\n        threadExited = true;\n    }\n\n    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {\n        RemoveThreadHangBuggyTest instance = new RemoveThreadHangBuggyTest();\n\n        Thread worker = new Thread(instance::workerThreadRun);\n        worker.start();\n\n        Thread remover = new Thread(instance::removeStreamThread);\n        remover.start();\n\n        remover.join();\n        worker.join();\n\n        if (!instance.threadExited) {\n            throw new AssertionError(\"Worker thread did not exit — lost wakeup!\");\n        }\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}

Here's a summary of the two concurrency bugs found and fixed:\n\n---\n\n## Bug 1: Deadlock — `stateListener.onChange()` called inside `synchronized(stateLock)`\n\n**File:** `KafkaStreams.java:345-379`\n\n**Root cause:** `KafkaStreams.setState()` invoked the user's `stateListener.onChange()` callback *inside* `synchronized(stateLock)`. This creates a classic A-B / B-A deadlock:\n- Thread A (`close()`): holds `this` monitor → calls `waitOnStates()` → waits on `stateLock`\n- Thread B (shutdown thread): holds `stateLock` → callback tries to acquire `this` monitor\n\n**Fix:** Moved the `stateListener.onChange()` call *outside* the `synchronized(stateLock)` block, matching the pattern already used in `StreamThread.setState()` and `GlobalStreamThread.setState()`.\n\n## Bug 2: Hang (Lost Wakeup) — `removeStreamThread()` missing `wakeupThreads()` call\n\n**File:** `KafkaStreams.java:1199`\n\n**Root cause:** `removeStreamThread()` calls `streamThread.shutdown()` which sets the thread's state to `PENDING_SHUTDOWN`, but does NOT call `topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads()`. If the target thread is blocked in `TopologyMetadata.maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()` (waiting on `topologyCV.await()`), it never wakes up to see its state changed, causing an indefinite hang. Note: `shutdownHelper()` (used by `close()`) correctly calls `wakeupThreads()` — the bug was only in the `removeStreamThread()` path.\n\n**Fix:** Added `topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads()` call immediately after `streamThread.shutdown()` in `removeStreamThread()`."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":340,"cache_read_input_tokens":119898,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":340,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"7476f412-4872-4f35-a0f3-27b26d2b1c70","uuid":"038f3ad9-3289-413b-bf77-a0736ab17913"}

This creates a classic A-B / B-A deadlock:\n- Thread A (`close()`): holds `this` monitor → calls `waitOnStates()` → waits on `stateLock`\n- Thread B (shutdown thread): holds `stateLock` → callback tries to acquire `this` monitor\n\n**Fix:** Moved the `stateListener.onChange()` call *outside* the `synchronized(stateLock)` block, matching the pattern already used in `StreamThread.setState()` and `GlobalStreamThread.setState()`.\n\n## Bug 2: Hang (Lost Wakeup) — `removeStreamThread()` missing `wakeupThreads()` call\n\n**File:** `KafkaStreams.java:1199`\n\n**Root cause:** `removeStreamThread()` calls `streamThread.shutdown()` which sets the thread's state to `PENDING_SHUTDOWN`, but does NOT call `topologyMetadata.wakeupThreads()`.
